dive_sites.name: Bajo el Cerro de la Cruz

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Nicolas Acuña, all rights reserved, uploaded by Nicolas Acuña

species.description_es: Tiburón pequeño y elegante con manchas rojizas sobre fondo gris. Se esconde en grietas rocosas durante el día y sale a cazar de noche. Es inofensivo y muy curioso con los buzos.
species.description_en: Small, slender shark with reddish-brown spots on a gray body. Hides in rocky crevices during the day and hunts at night. Harmless and often curious around divers.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Pat Webster @underwaterpat, all rights reserved, uploaded by Pat Webster @underwaterpat

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: El alga más grande del mundo, formando bosques submarinos impresionantes. Sus frondas pueden crecer hasta 45 metros desde el fondo. Es el hogar de cientos de especies marinas.
species.description_en: The world's largest seaweed, forming stunning underwater forests. Fronds can grow up to 45 meters from the seafloor. Home to hundreds of marine species.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Diego Mira San Martín, all rights reserved, uploaded by Diego Mira San Martín

additional_photos.attribution: no rights reserved
(c) Andrea Talone Rìo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Lucila Rajngewerc, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Chorito pequeño de color púrpura oscuro que forma densas colonias en las rocas intermareales. Es una de las especies más abundantes de la costa chilena. Sirve de alimento para estrellas y caracoles.
species.description_en: Small dark purple mussel forming dense colonies on intertidal rocks. One of the most abundant species on the Chilean coast. An important food source for sea stars and snails.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) MatiasG, some rights reserved (CC BY-ND), uploaded by MatiasG

species.description_es: Anémona grande y colorida que varía de verde a rojo intenso. Se encuentra adherida a rocas en pozas intermareales y zonas poco profundas. Sus tentáculos pueden picar levemente al contacto.
species.description_en: Large, colorful anemone ranging from green to deep red. Found attached to rocks in tide pools and shallow zones. Its tentacles can sting mildly on contact.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) matias_ma, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Quitón de textura granulosa con placas gris verdosas. Se adhiere firmemente a las rocas intermareales y raspa algas con su rádula. Es uno de los quitones más comunes de Chile central.
species.description_en: Chiton with granular texture and greenish-gray plates. Clings firmly to intertidal rocks and scrapes algae with its radula. One of the most common chitons in central Chile.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Lauren Martin, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Lauren Martin

species.description_es: Tiburón que se infla como globo cuando se siente amenazado, tragando agua para parecer más grande. Tiene manchas oscuras sobre fondo café claro. Descansa en cuevas rocosas durante el día.
species.description_en: A shark that inflates like a balloon when threatened by swallowing water to appear larger. Dark spots on a light brown body. Rests in rocky caves during the day.

species.common_name_es: Estrella de mar de cinco brazos

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Marcelo Andrés Rojas González, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Marcelo Andrés Rojas González

species.description_es: Estrella de mar de cinco brazos con colores que van del naranja al púrpura. Se encuentra en rocas y pozas intermareales, alimentándose de choritos. Es una de las estrellas más comunes de Chile.
species.description_en: Five-armed sea star ranging from orange to purple. Found on rocks and in tide pools, feeding on mussels. One of the most common sea stars in Chile.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) vicente_quinteros, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Erasmo Macaya Horta, some rights reserved (CC BY)
(c) Erasmo Macaya Horta, some rights reserved (CC BY)

species.description_es: Alga roja con frondas aplanadas en forma de cuchara, de color rojo oscuro a púrpura. Forma tapices densos en la zona intermareal baja. Se usa para producir carragenina.
species.description_en: Red alga with flattened spoon-shaped fronds, dark red to purple. Forms dense mats in the low intertidal zone. Used commercially to produce carrageenan.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Erasmo Macaya Horta, some rights reserved (CC BY), uploaded by Erasmo Macaya Horta

additional_photos.attribution: no rights reserved
(c) larsonek, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Mikel Zubimendi, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Lapa falsa con concha cónica de color gris con líneas radiales. A diferencia de las lapas verdaderas, respira aire. Muy común en las rocas intermareales de toda la costa chilena.
species.description_en: False limpet with a conical gray shell and radial lines. Unlike true limpets, it breathes air. Very common on intertidal rocks along the entire Chilean coast.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Gonzalo Bravo

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Daniel Fernando Vidal, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Daniel Fernando Vidal, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) sebu, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Estrella de mar pequeña y rechoncha de color naranja a rojo, con textura aterciopelada. Se encuentra bajo piedras y en grietas. Es carroñera y se alimenta de materia orgánica en descomposición.
species.description_en: Small, chunky sea star, orange to red with a velvety texture. Found under rocks and in crevices. A scavenger that feeds on decomposing organic matter.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Fernando Gracia, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Fernando Gracia

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Abel Romero, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Abel Romero, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Abel Romero, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Pulpo pequeño y tímido que cambia de color rápidamente entre rojo, marrón y blanco. Se esconde en grietas y conchas vacías. Muy común en las pozas de marea y zonas rocosas poco profundas.
species.description_en: Small, shy octopus that rapidly changes color between red, brown, and white. Hides in crevices and empty shells. Very common in tide pools and shallow rocky areas.
species.habitat: Rocky reefs, tide pools, under boulders

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Kat Halsey, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Kat Halsey

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Rodrigo Cesáreo Pampin, some rights reserved (CC BY)
(c) Violeta Lang, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Violeta Lang, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Alga calcárea ramificada de color rosado a blanquecino que parece un pequeño coral. Forma tapices duros y crujientes sobre las rocas. Crea microhábitats para pequeños invertebrados.
species.description_en: Branching calcareous alga, pink to whitish, resembling a small coral. Forms hard, crunchy mats on rocks. Creates microhabitats for small invertebrates.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) cstobie, some rights reserved (CC BY), uploaded by cstobie

species.description_es: La estrella de mar más grande de Chile, con hasta 40 brazos y colores vivos de naranja a púrpura. Puede medir más de 30 cm de diámetro. Es un depredador voraz de choritos y cirripedios.
species.description_en: Chile's largest sea star, with up to 40 arms and vivid orange to purple colors. Can exceed 30 cm in diameter. A voracious predator of mussels and barnacles.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) fabian soriano, all rights reserved, uploaded by fabian soriano

species.description_es: Caracolito diminuto con concha gris con franjas que recuerdan a un pijama. Vive en la zona supralitoral, casi fuera del agua, en grandes grupos. Resiste condiciones extremas de desecación.
species.description_en: Tiny snail with a gray shell and stripe pattern resembling pajamas. Lives in the supralittoral zone, almost out of water, in large groups. Survives extreme desiccation.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Erasmo Macaya Horta, some rights reserved (CC BY), uploaded by Erasmo Macaya Horta

species.description_es: Erizo de mar completamente negro con espinas cortas y gruesas. Común en fondos rocosos donde raspa algas. Es más pequeño que el erizo rojo comestible.
species.description_en: Completely black sea urchin with short, thick spines. Common on rocky bottoms where it grazes on algae. Smaller than the edible red sea urchin.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Shallow Marine Surveys Group, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Shallow Marine Surveys Group

additional_photos.attribution: (c) ceciastengo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Alma Nativa, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) fiorella_buglione, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Erizo de mar verde oliva a marrón con espinas delgadas y puntiagudas. Se encuentra en fondos rocosos y arenosos. A menudo se cubre con fragmentos de conchas y algas como camuflaje.
species.description_en: Olive-green to brown sea urchin with thin, pointed spines. Found on rocky and sandy bottoms. Often covers itself with shell fragments and algae for camouflage.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) maria_segura, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Cirripedio diminuto y cónico que forma costras blancas densas en las rocas más altas de la zona intermareal. Es el primer organismo que coloniza la roca después del supralitoral. Filtra alimento del agua cuando sube la marea.
species.description_en: Tiny conical barnacle forming dense white crusts on the highest intertidal rocks. The first organism to colonize rock below the supralittoral. Filter-feeds when the tide comes in.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Nicolas Olejnik, some rights reserved (CC BY), uploaded by Nicolas Olejnik

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Ulises Balza, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Daniel Fernando Vidal, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Daniel Fernando Vidal, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Estrella de mar delgada de color crema a rosado pálido con brazos largos y finos. Las hembras cuidan sus huevos debajo del cuerpo. Habita fondos rocosos en aguas frías del sur de Chile.
species.description_en: Slender sea star, cream to pale pink with long, thin arms. Females brood their eggs underneath their body. Inhabits rocky bottoms in cold southern Chilean waters.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Marcelo Andrés Rojas González, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Marcelo Andrés Rojas González

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Jonatan Ferrada, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Anémona pequeña y llamativa con tentáculos transparentes rayados de blanco y naranja. Forma colonias densas en paredes rocosas y bajo piedras. Una de las anémonas más bonitas de Chile.
species.description_en: Small, striking anemone with transparent tentacles striped white and orange. Forms dense colonies on rock walls and under boulders. One of Chile's prettiest anemones.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Neil McDaniel, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Neil McDaniel

species.description_es: Estrella de mar grande y robusta en forma de cojín, de color rojo anaranjado. Su superficie está cubierta de placas gruesas. Habita fondos profundos y se alimenta de esponjas y corales blandos.
species.description_en: Large, robust cushion-shaped sea star, orange-red in color. Surface covered with thick plates. Inhabits deep bottoms and feeds on sponges and soft corals.
species.habitat: Deep rocky bottoms, soft coral beds

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Gonzalo Bravo

additional_photos.attribution: (c) raulsstt, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Estrella de mar grande con 5-7 brazos largos de color verdoso a marrón con manchas claras. Muy común en los fiordos y canales del sur de Chile. Es un depredador activo de mejillones.
species.description_en: Large sea star with 5-7 long arms, greenish to brown with light spots. Very common in fjords and channels of southern Chile. An active predator of mussels.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Erasmo Macaya Horta, some rights reserved (CC BY)

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Erasmo Macaya Horta, some rights reserved (CC BY)

species.description_es: Nudibranquio blanco con pequeños tubérculos ásperos en el dorso y manchas marrones dispersas. Tiene branquias plumosas en la parte posterior. Se alimenta de esponjas en rocas poco profundas.
species.description_en: White nudibranch with small rough tubercles on its back and scattered brown spots. Has feathery gills at the rear. Feeds on sponges in shallow rocky areas.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Erasmo Macaya Horta, some rights reserved (CC BY), uploaded by Erasmo Macaya Horta

species.description_es: Lapa pequeña con un hermoso patrón de rayas blancas y negras como una cebra. Se encuentra adherida a rocas intermareales. Es endémica de la costa del Pacífico sudamericano.
species.description_en: Small limpet with a beautiful black and white zebra-stripe pattern. Found clinging to intertidal rocks. Endemic to the South American Pacific coast.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Femorale, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Lapa grande y gruesa con un orificio ovalado en la cima de la concha. Color gris verdoso con rayos oscuros. Es una de las fisurelas más grandes y comunes de Chile, apreciada como alimento.
species.description_en: Large, thick limpet with an oval opening at the shell's apex. Greenish-gray with dark rays. One of Chile's largest and most common keyhole limpets, valued as food.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Daniel Fernando Vidal, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Daniel Fernando Vidal

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY)
(c) Esteban Tazzioli, some rights reserved (CC BY)
(c) Esteban Tazzioli, some rights reserved (CC BY)

species.description_es: Nudibranquio grande y rechoncho cubierto de verrugas redondeadas, de color amarillo a marrón. Es el más grande de los nudibranquios chilenos. Se alimenta de esponjas en arrecifes rocosos.
species.description_en: Large, chunky nudibranch covered in rounded warts, yellow to brown in color. The largest Chilean nudibranch. Feeds on sponges on rocky reefs.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Fernando Gracia, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Fernando Gracia, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Nudibranquio espectacular con ceratas naranjas y rojos en el dorso que parecen llamas de fuego. Tiene una línea azul brillante en la cabeza. Uno de los nudibranquios más fotografiados de Chile.
species.description_en: Spectacular nudibranch with orange and red cerata on its back resembling flames. Has a bright blue line on its head. One of Chile's most photographed nudibranchs.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Ignacio Contreras, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Ignacio Contreras

species.description_es: Raya de fondo con cuerpo aplanado y textura áspera como lija. Color café con manchas oscuras. Se entierra en la arena cerca de arrecifes rocosos y es inofensiva para los buzos.
species.description_en: Bottom-dwelling ray with a flattened body and rough sandpaper-like texture. Brown with dark spots. Buries in sand near rocky reefs and is harmless to divers.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) pablo_zamora, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY)
(c) Antonella, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Antonella, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Nudibranquio blanco cremoso con pequeños puntos negros dispersos por el dorso. Tiene cerdas o papilas cortas que le dan textura. Se encuentra bajo piedras alimentándose de esponjas.
species.description_en: Creamy white nudibranch with small black dots scattered across its back. Has short bristle-like papillae giving it texture. Found under rocks feeding on sponges.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Jurga Motiejūnaitė, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Jurga Motiejūnaitė

species.description_es: Cangrejo de colores vivos — rojo, naranja y azul — que corre velozmente por las rocas costeras. Los juveniles son oscuros y se camuflan perfectamente. Es muy ágil y difícil de atrapar.
species.description_en: Vividly colored crab — red, orange, and blue — that sprints across coastal rocks. Juveniles are dark and perfectly camouflaged. Very agile and hard to catch.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) artzerphotog, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Leonardo Adrián LEIVA, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Ulises Balza, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Pablo, some rights reserved (CC BY)

species.description_es: Alga verde brillante con láminas translúcidas que parecen hojas de lechuga. Muy común en pozas intermareales y aguas poco profundas. Indicadora de aguas ricas en nutrientes.
species.description_en: Bright green alga with translucent sheets resembling lettuce leaves. Very common in tide pools and shallow waters. An indicator of nutrient-rich waters.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Thorny Toad Photography, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Thorny Toad Photography

species.description_es: Crustáceo ovalado que vive enterrado en la arena de las playas, filtrando alimento de las olas. Se desplaza rápidamente enterrándose hacia atrás. Es un indicador de la salud de las playas.
species.description_en: Oval crustacean that lives buried in beach sand, filter-feeding from waves. Quickly burrows backward into the sand. An indicator of beach ecosystem health.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Saryu Mae 前 朝琉, some rights reserved (CC BY), uploaded by Saryu Mae 前 朝琉

species.description_es: Cangrejo grande de color púrpura a violeta con patas largas y aplanadas. Corre con agilidad por las rocas costeras. Es nocturno y se alimenta de algas e invertebrados pequeños.
species.description_en: Large purple to violet crab with long, flattened legs. Runs nimbly across coastal rocks. Nocturnal, feeding on algae and small invertebrates.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Thomas Quine, some rights reserved (CC BY)

species.description_es: Cangrejo ermitaño terrestre que usa conchas de caracol como casa portátil. Tiene ojos grandes y patas rugosas. Muy común en playas tropicales donde busca restos orgánicos de noche.
species.description_en: Land hermit crab that uses snail shells as a portable home. Has large eyes and rugose legs. Common on tropical beaches where it scavenges organic debris at night.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Rich Kostecke, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Rich Kostecke

species.description_es: Cangrejo de playa de color arena con ojos prominentes en forma de maza. Cava madrigueras profundas en la arena y es muy rápido al correr. Más activo al atardecer y de noche.
species.description_en: Sand-colored beach crab with prominent club-shaped eyes. Digs deep burrows in the sand and runs very fast. Most active at dusk and nighttime.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Elí García-Padilla, all rights reserved, uploaded by Elí García-Padilla

species.description_es: Cangrejo fantasma de color blanco cremoso que se mimetiza con la arena. Tiene ojos en forma de cuerno y una pinza más grande que la otra. Corre a gran velocidad por la playa.
species.description_en: Creamy white ghost crab that blends perfectly with sand. Has horn-shaped eyes and one claw larger than the other. Runs at impressive speed across the beach.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Ari & Mac, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Ari & Mac

species.description_es: Cangrejo pequeño de color verdoso a café con manchas blancas y pinzas peludas. Muy común bajo piedras en el intermareal. Es omnívoro y se alimenta de algas y pequeños invertebrados.
species.description_en: Small greenish to brown crab with white spots and hairy claws. Very common under rocks in the intertidal zone. Omnivorous, feeding on algae and small invertebrates.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) tangatawhenua, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by tangatawhenua

species.description_es: Cangrejito diminuto que vive sobre objetos flotantes como troncos, boyas y hasta tortugas marinas. Su color varía según el sustrato donde habita. Un verdadero viajero oceánico.
species.description_en: Tiny crab that lives on floating objects like logs, buoys, and even sea turtles. Its color varies depending on its substrate. A true oceanic traveler.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) MatiasG, some rights reserved (CC BY-ND), uploaded by MatiasG

species.description_es: Anémona robusta de colores variables — roja, verde, marrón — con papilas en la columna. Muy similar a P. clematis y común en rocas intermareales. Sus tentáculos capturan pequeños crustáceos.
species.description_en: Robust anemone with variable colors — red, green, brown — and papillae on the column. Very similar to P. clematis and common on intertidal rocks. Tentacles capture small crustaceans.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Claudio Maureira, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC-SA), uploaded by Claudio Maureira

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Daniel Fernando Vidal, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Daniel Fernando Vidal, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Anémona pequeña de color café verdoso que se reproduce tanto sexual como asexualmente. Forma grupos clonales densos en grietas rocosas. Es hermafrodita, lo que facilita su reproducción.
species.description_en: Small brownish-green anemone that reproduces both sexually and asexually. Forms dense clonal groups in rock crevices. It's hermaphroditic, facilitating reproduction.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Nicolas Olejnik, some rights reserved (CC BY), uploaded by Nicolas Olejnik

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Leonel Roget, some rights reserved (CC BY)
(c) Leonel Roget, some rights reserved (CC BY)
(c) Tadeo Gringras Custer, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Jaiba nadadora con tres manchas violetas en el caparazón y patas traseras aplanadas como remos. Se entierra en la arena y nada activamente. Tiene un sabor excelente y es muy apreciada.
species.description_en: Swimming crab with three violet spots on the carapace and flattened paddle-like rear legs. Buries in sand and actively swims. Highly valued for its excellent flavor.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Mariano Rodriguez, all rights reserved, uploaded by Mariano Rodriguez

additional_photos.attribution: no rights reserved
(c) Carolina Pantano, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Eloísa GI, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC-SA)

species.description_es: Caracol depredador con un diente prominente en la apertura de la concha que usa para perforar presas. Color gris oscuro a negro. Se alimenta de cirripedios y choritos en las rocas.
species.description_en: Predatory snail with a prominent tooth on the shell opening used to drill into prey. Dark gray to black. Feeds on barnacles and mussels on rocks.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Duban Espinoza, some rights reserved (CC BY), uploaded by Duban Espinoza

species.description_es: Jaiba grande de color negro violáceo con pinzas poderosas y lisas. Es la jaiba más apreciada gastronómicamente en Chile. Se encuentra bajo rocas grandes y en grietas submarinas.
species.description_en: Large blackish-violet crab with powerful, smooth claws. The most prized crab for eating in Chile. Found under large rocks and in underwater crevices.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) gaston-fernandez, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Carolina Pantano, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Caracol cónico de color negro intenso y brillante. Se mueve lentamente por las rocas raspando algas. Muy común en el intermareal bajo y pozas de marea de Chile central.
species.description_en: Conical snail with an intense, shiny black shell. Moves slowly over rocks grazing algae. Very common in the low intertidal and tide pools of central Chile.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) César Pedrini, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by César Pedrini

species.description_es: El famoso 'loco' chileno — un caracol grande con concha gruesa y aplanada. Es un depredador voraz de cirripedios y choritos. Protegido por vedas debido a su sobreexplotación histórica.
species.description_en: The famous Chilean 'loco' — a large snail with a thick, flattened shell. A voracious predator of barnacles and mussels. Protected by fishing bans due to historical overharvesting.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Paula González Valderrama, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Paula González Valderrama

species.description_es: Cangrejo araña grande de color rojo a marrón que vive entre los bosques de kelp. Tiene caparazón triangular con espinas y patas largas. Se camufla con algas y organismos que crecen sobre él.
species.description_en: Large spider crab, red to brown, living among kelp forests. Has a triangular spiny carapace and long legs. Camouflages itself with algae and organisms growing on its shell.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Fernando Gracia, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Fernando Gracia

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Talía Salomón, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Talía Salomón, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Talía Salomón, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: El cirripedio más grande del mundo, alcanzando hasta 30 cm. Parece un volcán blanco rosado adherido a las rocas. Es un manjar de la gastronomía chilena, conocido como picoroco.
species.description_en: The world's largest barnacle, reaching up to 30 cm. Looks like a pinkish-white volcano attached to rocks. A delicacy in Chilean cuisine, known as 'picoroco'.

primary_photo.attribution: no rights reserved, uploaded by Hugo Hulsberg

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Pablo Fraire, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
no rights reserved
no rights reserved

species.description_es: Cangrejo pequeño de color grisáceo a verdoso con caparazón anguloso. Habita estuarios y zonas intermareales con agua salobre. Es tolerante a amplias variaciones de salinidad.
species.description_en: Small grayish to greenish crab with an angular carapace. Inhabits estuaries and intertidal areas with brackish water. Tolerant of wide salinity variations.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Dominic Lizana, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Dominic Lizana

species.description_es: Cangrejo peludo de color rojizo cubierto de sedas suaves. Tiene pinzas robustas con puntas negras. Se esconde bajo rocas y en grietas durante el día, más activo de noche.
species.description_en: Reddish hairy crab covered in soft bristles. Has robust claws with black tips. Hides under rocks and in crevices during the day, more active at night.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Daniel Fernando Vidal, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Daniel Fernando Vidal

additional_photos.attribution: (c) martinhalliburton, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Anémona con base lobulada y tentáculos blancos a rosados. Su columna tiene verrugas adhesivas donde pega arena y fragmentos. Común en paredes rocosas y bajo piedras.
species.description_en: Anemone with a lobed base and white to pinkish tentacles. Its column has adhesive warts where it sticks sand and fragments. Common on rock walls and under boulders.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) marcelo_mt, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Rachel Crane, some rights reserved (CC BY)
(c) Carolina Pantano, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Quitón grande y llamativo con placas verdes oscuras rodeadas por un cinturón colorido con granulaciones. El más grande de los quitones chilenos. Se encuentra en el intermareal bajo.
species.description_en: Large, striking chiton with dark green plates surrounded by a colorful granulated girdle. Chile's largest chiton. Found in the low intertidal zone.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Eduardo Muñoz Orellana, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Eduardo Muñoz Orellana

species.description_es: Cangrejito porcelana de color violeta intenso con pinzas aplanadas. Filtra alimento del agua con sus maxilípedos plumosos. Se encuentra bajo piedras en el intermareal.
species.description_en: Porcelain crab of intense violet color with flattened claws. Filter-feeds from water using its feathery maxillipeds. Found under rocks in the intertidal zone.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Nicolas Olejnik, some rights reserved (CC BY), uploaded by Nicolas Olejnik

additional_photos.attribution: (c) ianwalker, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
no rights reserved
no rights reserved

species.description_es: Cangrejo redondo y aplanado con caparazón espinoso de color naranja a rojo. Se arrastra lentamente por fondos arenosos y fangosos. Tiene una apariencia muy particular, como un tractor en miniatura.
species.description_en: Round, flattened crab with a spiny orange to red carapace. Crawls slowly across sandy and muddy bottoms. Has a very distinctive appearance, like a miniature tractor.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Juan Miguel Cancino, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Juan Miguel Cancino

species.description_es: Anémona que se entierra parcialmente en arena con solo los tentáculos expuestos. Tiene columna cubierta de granos de arena adheridos. Los tentáculos son grises a marrones con bandas.
species.description_en: Anemone that partially buries in sand with only tentacles exposed. Has a column covered with adhered sand grains. Tentacles are gray to brown with bands.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Camilla Caton, all rights reserved, uploaded by Camilla Caton

species.description_es: Pez largo y plateado con cuerpo aerodinámico y mandíbula con dientes afilados. Es un depredador pelágico rápido que caza en cardúmenes. Apreciado en la pesca deportiva chilena.
species.description_en: Long, silvery fish with a streamlined body and sharp-toothed jaw. A fast pelagic predator that hunts in schools. Valued in Chilean sport fishing.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) natalia_vera, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by natalia_vera

species.description_es: Cangrejo pequeño y aplanado de color gris ceniza. Vive bajo piedras en la zona intermareal alta. Es muy discreto y se queda inmóvil cuando se levanta su piedra.
species.description_en: Small, flattened ash-gray crab. Lives under rocks in the upper intertidal zone. Very discreet and stays motionless when its rock is lifted.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Gonzalo Bravo

species.description_es: Cangrejo ermitaño con patas rayadas de rojo y blanco que habita conchas vacías de caracol. Se ve frecuentemente caminando por fondos rocosos y arenosos. Cambia de concha a medida que crece.
species.description_en: Hermit crab with red and white striped legs that inhabits empty snail shells. Frequently seen walking across rocky and sandy bottoms. Changes shells as it grows.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Marcelo Andrés Rojas González, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Marcelo Andrés Rojas González

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Cecilia Paula Alonso, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Cecilia Paula Alonso, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Rachel Crane, some rights reserved (CC BY)

species.description_es: Quitón de placas variables en color — verdes, marrones o rojizas — con cinturón liso. Se adhiere firmemente a rocas intermareales y raspa algas. Común en toda la costa de Chile central.
species.description_en: Chiton with variable-colored plates — green, brown, or reddish — and a smooth girdle. Clings firmly to intertidal rocks and grazes algae. Common throughout central Chile's coast.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) jorgelaras, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Jaiba de caparazón rojizo a anaranjado con bordes dentados. Es una de las jaibas más comunes de Chile y se captura artesanalmente. Se encuentra en fondos rocosos y arenosos.
species.description_en: Crab with a reddish to orange carapace with toothed edges. One of Chile's most common crabs, caught by artisanal fishers. Found on rocky and sandy bottoms.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Erasmo Macaya Horta, some rights reserved (CC BY), uploaded by Erasmo Macaya Horta

species.description_es: Alga parda grande con estipes gruesos y rígidos que forman bosques submarinos densos. Es la especie que define el paisaje submarino rocoso de Chile. Alberga una enorme biodiversidad.
species.description_en: Large brown alga with thick, rigid stipes forming dense underwater forests. Defines the rocky submarine landscape of Chile. Harbors enormous biodiversity.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Wayne Martin, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Wayne Martin

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Pablo, some rights reserved (CC BY)
(c) Pablo, some rights reserved (CC BY)

species.description_es: Alga roja filamentosa y delicada con ramificaciones dicotómicas y puntas curvadas como ganchos. Forma matas suaves y sedosas sobre rocas y otras algas. Muy común pero fácil de pasar por alto.
species.description_en: Delicate filamentous red alga with dichotomous branching and hook-like curved tips. Forms soft, silky tufts on rocks and other algae. Very common but easy to overlook.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Marcelo Andrés Rojas González, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Marcelo Andrés Rojas González

species.description_es: Camarón colorido con cuerpo rayado rojo y blanco y un rostro largo y móvil. Vive en grietas y cuevas rocosas en grupos grandes. De noche sale a buscar alimento, iluminado por la linterna del buzo.
species.description_en: Colorful shrimp with a red and white striped body and a long, movable rostrum. Lives in rocky crevices and caves in large groups. Comes out to feed at night, lit up by a diver's torch.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Paula González Valderrama, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Paula González Valderrama

species.description_es: Pez rechoncho con ventosa ventral que se adhiere firmemente a las rocas del intermareal. Color rojizo a gris con piel lisa y resbaladiza. Puede respirar aire y sobrevivir fuera del agua.
species.description_en: Chunky fish with a ventral suction cup that clings firmly to intertidal rocks. Reddish to gray with smooth, slippery skin. Can breathe air and survive out of water.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) matias saa, all rights reserved, uploaded by matias saa

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Ulises Balza, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Lapa pequeña de concha cónica baja con costillas radiales y color gris verdoso. Vive sobre el alga Lessonia, creando una cicatriz en el estipe. Relación específica con su alga hospedera.
species.description_en: Small limpet with a low conical shell, radial ribs, and greenish-gray color. Lives on Lessonia kelp, creating a scar on the stipe. Has a specific relationship with its host alga.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) sebu, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Martin Brogger, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Martin Brogger, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Daniel Fernando Vidal, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Pez robusto de color rojo anaranjado con ojos grandes y cabeza espinosa. Habita cuevas y grietas en arrecifes rocosos profundos. Es solitario y territorial, permaneciendo quieto entre las rocas.
species.description_en: Robust orange-red fish with large eyes and a spiny head. Inhabits caves and crevices in deep rocky reefs. Solitary and territorial, staying still among the rocks.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Claudio Maureira, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC-SA), uploaded by Claudio Maureira

species.description_es: Jaiba de caparazón ovalado con pelos densos en las patas y pinzas. Color café rojizo a morado. Se esconde en grietas y bajo piedras grandes en el submareal somero.
species.description_en: Crab with an oval carapace and dense hair on legs and claws. Reddish-brown to purple. Hides in crevices and under large rocks in the shallow subtidal.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Shallow Marine Surveys Group, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Shallow Marine Surveys Group

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Lucas Pereira, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Lucas Pereira, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Lucas Pereira, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Camarón elegante de cuerpo transparente con bandas rojas y blancas. Habita aguas profundas y frías del sur de Chile. Su cuerpo curvado le da un perfil jorobado característico.
species.description_en: Elegant shrimp with a transparent body and red and white bands. Inhabits deep, cold waters of southern Chile. Its curved body gives it a characteristic humped profile.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Leonardo Mondaca Lara, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Leonardo Mondaca Lara

additional_photos.attribution: no rights reserved
no rights reserved
no rights reserved

species.description_es: Cangrejito diminuto y aplanado de color verdoso a café. Se encuentra entre algas, bajo piedras y en conchas de mejillones. Es uno de los cangrejos más pequeños de las costas chilenas.
species.description_en: Tiny, flattened crab, greenish to brown. Found among algae, under rocks, and in mussel shells. One of the smallest crabs on Chilean coasts.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) KJ, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by KJ

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Cecilia Paula Alonso, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Cecilia Paula Alonso, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Estrella de mar pentagonal y aplanada que parece una galleta, de color naranja a rojo. Su superficie es lisa y firme. Habita fondos profundos y se mueve muy lentamente.
species.description_en: Pentagonal, flattened sea star resembling a cookie, orange to red. Surface is smooth and firm. Inhabits deep bottoms and moves very slowly.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) dschr, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by dschr

species.description_es: Cangrejo caja con caparazón ovalado y patas delanteras armadas con espinas. Color amarillento con manchas rojas. Se entierra en arena y usa sus pinzas para defenderse cerrándose como una caja.
species.description_en: Box crab with an oval carapace and forelegs armed with spines. Yellowish with red spots. Buries in sand and uses its claws defensively, closing up like a box.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Vuelve al Océano, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Vuelve al Océano

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Abel Romero, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Abel Romero, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY)

species.description_es: Nudibranquio pequeño y delicado de color blanco translúcido con puntos amarillos. Se desliza suavemente sobre esponjas de las que se alimenta. Requiere ojo atento para encontrarlo.
species.description_en: Small, delicate nudibranch, translucent white with yellow dots. Glides smoothly over the sponges it feeds on. Requires a keen eye to spot.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Paula González Valderrama, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Paula González Valderrama

species.description_es: Pez robusto de color café grisáceo con cabeza grande y labios gruesos. Permanece cerca del fondo en arrecifes rocosos y es curioso con los buzos. Se alimenta de invertebrados bentónicos.
species.description_en: Robust grayish-brown fish with a large head and thick lips. Stays near the bottom on rocky reefs and is curious around divers. Feeds on benthic invertebrates.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Gonzalo Bravo

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Daniel Fernando Vidal, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Daniel Fernando Vidal, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Daniel Fernando Vidal, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Estrella de mar con textura reticulada y espinas cortas, de color rojo a naranja. Sus brazos son relativamente cortos y gruesos. Se encuentra en fondos rocosos a profundidades moderadas.
species.description_en: Sea star with reticulated texture and short spines, red to orange. Arms are relatively short and thick. Found on rocky bottoms at moderate depths.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Kala Murphy King, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC-ND), uploaded by Kala Murphy King

additional_photos.attribution: (c) lucianv, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) lucianv, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) lucianv, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Cormorán completamente negro con pico en forma de gancho y piel naranja en la garganta. Se posa en rocas costeras con las alas abiertas para secarse. Excelente buceador que persigue peces bajo el agua.
species.description_en: All-black cormorant with a hooked bill and orange throat skin. Perches on coastal rocks with wings spread to dry. Excellent diver that pursues fish underwater.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Sebastián Lescano, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Sebastián Lescano

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Eduardo Luis Beltrocco, some rights reserved (CC BY)
(c) Alejandro Duvieilh, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) COA Lobería., some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Gaviota grande con dorso negro, vientre blanco y pico amarillo con punto rojo. Es la gaviota más común y grande de Chile. Oportunista y carroñera, se ve en puertos y playas.
species.description_en: Large gull with a black back, white belly, and yellow bill with red spot. Chile's most common and largest gull. Opportunistic scavenger, seen at ports and beaches.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Christine Morrow, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Christine Morrow

additional_photos.attribution: (c) facundo_saleme, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Mejillón de concha negra azulada alargada que forma densos bancos en rocas y estructuras costeras. Es filtrador y limpia el agua eficientemente. Importante tanto ecológica como gastronómicamente.
species.description_en: Mussel with an elongated blue-black shell forming dense beds on rocks and coastal structures. A filter feeder that efficiently cleans water. Important both ecologically and gastronomically.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Jason M Crockwell, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC-ND), uploaded by Jason M Crockwell

species.description_es: Caracol con concha aplanada que se apila sobre otros individuos formando cadenas. Los de abajo son hembras y los de arriba machos. Especie invasora originaria de Norteamérica.
species.description_en: Snail with a flat shell that stacks on top of others forming chains. Bottom ones are female and top ones male. Invasive species originally from North America.

additional_photos.attribution: (c) flaviobruno, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) nicochimento, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Maximiliano Aguilar, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Gaviota migratoria de tamaño mediano con capucha negra en temporada reproductiva y cuerpo gris claro. Llega a Chile desde Norteamérica durante el verano austral. Se ve en grandes bandadas.
species.description_en: Medium-sized migratory gull with a black hood in breeding season and light gray body. Arrives in Chile from North America during the austral summer. Seen in large flocks.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) David Marvin, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC-ND)

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Emily Christian, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Emily Christian, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Emily Christian, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Pingüino mediano con dos bandas negras en el pecho y cara blanca con antifaz negro. Nidifica en madrigueras en la costa. Excelente nadador que puede sumergirse más de 50 metros.
species.description_en: Medium penguin with two black chest bands and a white face with black mask. Nests in burrows along the coast. Excellent swimmer that can dive over 50 meters deep.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Ian Banks, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Ian Banks

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Nacho Pavía, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Nacho Pavía, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Pez plateado alargado con rayas horizontales oscuras y cabeza ancha y aplanada. Nada en cardúmenes cerca de la superficie en aguas costeras. Se alimenta filtrando microalgas del fondo.
species.description_en: Elongated silvery fish with dark horizontal stripes and a broad, flat head. Swims in schools near the surface in coastal waters. Feeds by filtering microalgae from the bottom.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Oscar Thomas, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Oscar Thomas

additional_photos.attribution: no rights reserved
(c) Martin Arregui, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
no rights reserved

species.description_es: Ave marina de color gris oscuro uniforme con vuelo rasante sobre las olas. Migra miles de kilómetros entre hemisferios. Se zambulle desde el aire para capturar peces y calamares.
species.description_en: Uniformly dark gray seabird with low skimming flight over waves. Migrates thousands of kilometers between hemispheres. Plunge-dives from the air to catch fish and squid.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) aeb95f, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Martin Arregui, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Gabriel Baño, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
no rights reserved

species.description_es: Albatros elegante con ceja negra sobre ojos oscuros, dorso gris oscuro y vientre blanco. Tiene una envergadura de más de 2 metros. Planea sobre el viento sin esfuerzo durante horas.
species.description_en: Elegant albatross with a black eyebrow over dark eyes, dark gray back, and white belly. Wingspan over 2 meters. Glides effortlessly on the wind for hours.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Paul Steeves, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Paul Steeves

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Julieta Ameglio, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Paul G. Schrijvershof, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC-ND)

species.description_es: Petrel enorme de color gris oscuro a marrón con pico macizo y amarillento. Es carroñero y depredador, con una envergadura de hasta 2 metros. Se le ve siguiendo barcos pesqueros.
species.description_en: Enormous petrel, dark gray to brown with a massive yellowish bill. Both a scavenger and predator, with a wingspan up to 2 meters. Often follows fishing boats.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Dan Harville, some rights reserved (CC BY-SA), uploaded by Dan Harville

species.description_es: Pelícano grande de color café oscuro con bolsa gular prominente. Se zambulle desde el aire para atrapar peces. Es endémico de la corriente de Humboldt y muy común en puertos chilenos.
species.description_en: Large dark brown pelican with a prominent throat pouch. Plunge-dives from the air to catch fish. Endemic to the Humboldt Current and very common in Chilean ports.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Gabriel Barathieu, some rights reserved (CC BY-SA)

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Enzo Bonanno, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Enzo Bonanno, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Enzo Bonanno, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: El mayor depredador con dientes del planeta, con cabeza cuadrada que ocupa un tercio de su cuerpo. Puede sumergirse a más de 2000 metros para cazar calamares gigantes. Su soplo es inclinado hacia la izquierda.
species.description_en: The largest toothed predator on Earth, with a square head making up a third of its body. Can dive over 2000 meters to hunt giant squid. Its blow angles to the left.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Titouan Roguet, all rights reserved, uploaded by Titouan Roguet

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Ezequiel Vera, some rights reserved (CC BY)
(c) Ezequiel Vera, some rights reserved (CC BY)
(c) Ezequiel Vera, some rights reserved (CC BY)

species.description_es: Ave marina diminuta de color oscuro que parece caminar sobre el agua mientras se alimenta. Tiene una mancha blanca en la rabadilla. Es una de las aves más abundantes del mundo.
species.description_en: Tiny dark seabird that appears to walk on water while feeding. Has a white rump patch. One of the most abundant birds in the world.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Robin Gwen Agarwal, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Robin Gwen Agarwal

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Tomás Tamagno, some rights reserved (CC BY)
(c) Tomás Tamagno, some rights reserved (CC BY)
(c) Tomás Tamagno, some rights reserved (CC BY)

species.description_es: Delfín grande de color gris que se vuelve casi blanco con la edad por cicatrices de interacciones sociales. Tiene cabeza redondeada sin hocico pronunciado. Viaja en grupos de 10 a 30 individuos.
species.description_en: Large gray dolphin that turns nearly white with age from social interaction scars. Has a rounded head without a pronounced beak. Travels in groups of 10 to 30.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Jonathan Newman, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Jonathan Newman

species.description_es: El ave con la mayor envergadura del mundo, alcanzando hasta 3.5 metros. Cuerpo blanco con puntas de alas negras. Puede planear durante horas sin aletear, recorriendo miles de kilómetros.
species.description_en: The bird with the largest wingspan in the world, reaching up to 3.5 meters. White body with black wingtips. Can glide for hours without flapping, covering thousands of kilometers.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Steve Murray, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Steve Murray

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Martin Arregui, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
no rights reserved
(c) Edgar Romeo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Petrel de tamaño mediano con plumaje blanco y negro moteado como un tablero de damas. Sigue barcos y se alimenta de krill y restos de pesca. Común en aguas subantárticas chilenas.
species.description_en: Medium-sized petrel with checkered black and white plumage like a checkerboard. Follows ships and feeds on krill and fishing scraps. Common in Chilean sub-Antarctic waters.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) martinmoscovich, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

additional_photos.attribution: (c) martinmoscovich, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Ballena grande y robusta de color negro con callosidades blancas únicas en la cabeza. No tiene aleta dorsal. Llega a las costas chilenas para reproducirse en invierno. Muy dócil y curiosa.
species.description_en: Large, robust black whale with unique white callosities on the head. No dorsal fin. Arrives at Chilean coasts to breed in winter. Very gentle and curious.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Christoph Moning, some rights reserved (CC BY), uploaded by Christoph Moning

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Guille Ivan Spajic, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) El Naturalista, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) El Naturalista, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Petrel grande de color café oscuro uniforme con pico amarillento y barbilla blanca. Sigue barcos pesqueros y se alimenta de calamares y peces. Habita aguas subantárticas y templadas.
species.description_en: Large uniformly dark brown petrel with a yellowish bill and white chin. Follows fishing boats and feeds on squid and fish. Inhabits sub-Antarctic and temperate waters.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Jay Keller, all rights reserved, uploaded by Jay Keller

species.description_es: Lapa blanca con forma de gorro cónico alto. Su concha es lisa y calcárea, casi sin escultura. Se encuentra en zonas sublitorales sobre rocas cubiertas de algas coralinas.
species.description_en: White limpet with a tall conical cap shape. Shell is smooth and calcareous, almost without sculpture. Found in subtidal zones on rocks covered with coralline algae.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Paul Fenwick, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Paul Fenwick

species.description_es: Cormorán grande de color negro con piel facial roja y garganta desnuda. Anida en colonias enormes en islas guaneras. Sus excrementos fueron la base de la industria del guano.
species.description_en: Large black cormorant with red facial skin and bare throat. Nests in enormous colonies on guano islands. Its droppings were the foundation of the guano industry.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Luca Boscain, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Luca Boscain

additional_photos.attribution: (c) sical-isfla-veola, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) sical-isfla-veola, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) sical-isfla-veola, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Cormorán gris elegante con patas rojas brillantes y parches blancos en los flancos. Es el cormorán más bonito de Chile. Anida en acantilados rocosos y pesca en aguas costeras.
species.description_en: Elegant gray cormorant with bright red legs and white flank patches. Chile's most beautiful cormorant. Nests on rocky cliffs and fishes in coastal waters.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) LUPRA, all rights reserved, uploaded by LUPRA

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Pablo, some rights reserved (CC BY)
(c) Pablo, some rights reserved (CC BY)
(c) Pablo, some rights reserved (CC BY)

species.description_es: Alga parda gigante conocida como cochayuyo, con estipes gruesos y frondas que flotan gracias a su estructura tipo panal. Es comestible y forma parte de la gastronomía chilena tradicional.
species.description_en: Giant brown alga known as cochayuyo, with thick stipes and fronds that float thanks to a honeycomb-like structure. Edible and part of traditional Chilean cuisine.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Frédéric ANDRE, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Frédéric ANDRE

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Pete Woodall, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Alga parda con forma de tubo largo y constreñido a intervalos, como una salchicha. Color café oliváceo. Crece en pozas intermareales y rocas poco profundas en primavera y verano.
species.description_en: Brown alga shaped like a long tube constricted at intervals, like a sausage. Olive-brown color. Grows in tide pools and shallow rocks in spring and summer.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Shaun Lee, some rights reserved (CC BY-SA)

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Martin Arregui, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Martin Arregui, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Michelle Delaloye, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Pez pequeño y alargado de color plateado con manchas oscuras. Vive tanto en agua dulce como salada, migrando al mar para reproducirse. Es una especie clave en los ríos y estuarios chilenos.
species.description_en: Small, elongated silvery fish with dark spots. Lives in both fresh and salt water, migrating to sea to breed. A key species in Chilean rivers and estuaries.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) David F. Belmonte, some rights reserved (CC BY), uploaded by David F. Belmonte

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Diego Trillo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Diego Trillo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Diego Trillo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Ave rapaz marina agresiva de color café oscuro con capucha negra. Acosa a otras aves para robarles la comida. Es territorial y defiende su nido atacando a cualquier intruso.
species.description_en: Aggressive dark brown marine raptor with a black hood. Harasses other birds to steal their food. Territorial and defends its nest by attacking any intruder.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Marc Henrion, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Marc Henrion

additional_photos.attribution: (c) COA Lobería., some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Eduardo Luis Beltrocco, some rights reserved (CC BY)
(c) Eduardo Luis Beltrocco, some rights reserved (CC BY)

species.description_es: Petrel de color gris plateado pálido con alas oscuras y pico rosado. Vuela con planeos rígidos sobre las olas. Es una especie antártica que llega a aguas chilenas durante el invierno.
species.description_en: Pale silvery-gray petrel with dark wings and a pinkish bill. Flies with stiff glides over waves. An Antarctic species that reaches Chilean waters during winter.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) James Peake, all rights reserved, uploaded by James Peake

species.description_es: Pepino de mar grande y blando de color marrón oscuro con textura rugosa. Se arrastra lentamente por fondos arenosos y rocosos reciclando materia orgánica. Importante reciclador del fondo marino.
species.description_en: Large, soft sea cucumber, dark brown with a rough texture. Crawls slowly across sandy and rocky bottoms recycling organic matter. An important seafloor recycler.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Gonzalo Bravo

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Leyla Cuello, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Leyla Cuello, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Lucila Rajngewerc, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Mejillón grande de concha oscura con costillas radiales marcadas. Forma bancos densos en rocas sublitorales. Es muy apreciado como mariscos en Chile, conocido como cholga.
species.description_en: Large mussel with a dark shell and prominent radial ribs. Forms dense beds on subtidal rocks. Highly prized as seafood in Chile, known as 'cholga'.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) catta, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Quitón robusto de color café oscuro con placas anchas y cinturón ancho cubierto de escamas. Se encuentra en el intermareal bajo, adherido firmemente a las rocas.
species.description_en: Robust dark brown chiton with wide plates and a broad girdle covered in scales. Found in the low intertidal zone, firmly attached to rocks.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Txalen Galina-Gaiton, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Txalen Galina-Gaiton

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Nicolas Olejnik, some rights reserved (CC BY)

species.description_es: Pez alargado de color gris plateado con boca grande y dientes afilados. Es un depredador voraz que caza en aguas medias y profundas. Muy importante para la pesca comercial en Chile.
species.description_en: Elongated silvery-gray fish with a large mouth and sharp teeth. A voracious predator hunting in mid and deep waters. Very important for commercial fishing in Chile.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Donna Pomeroy, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Donna Pomeroy

species.description_es: Lapa diminuta que vive exclusivamente sobre las frondas de algas pardas. Su concha es aplanada y de color marrón oscuro. Crea cicatrices en el alga donde se asienta.
species.description_en: Tiny limpet that lives exclusively on brown algae fronds. Its shell is flattened and dark brown. Creates scars on the algae where it settles.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Carlos Gonzalez, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Carlos Gonzalez

species.description_es: Anémona de color rojo intenso a naranja con tentáculos largos y ondulantes. Se reproduce rápidamente y puede cubrir grandes superficies rocosas. Es una especie invasora en la costa chilena.
species.description_en: Bright red to orange anemone with long, flowing tentacles. Reproduces rapidly and can cover large rock surfaces. An invasive species on the Chilean coast.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) leo_in_merimbula, all rights reserved

additional_photos.attribution: no rights reserved
(c) Michelle Delaloye, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
no rights reserved

species.description_es: Albatros de tamaño mediano con pico negro y línea amarilla en la cresta del pico. Plumaje blanco con dorso gris oscuro. Visitante ocasional en aguas chilenas durante su migración.
species.description_en: Medium-sized albatross with a black bill and yellow line along the bill crest. White plumage with dark gray back. Occasional visitor to Chilean waters during migration.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) BJ Stacey, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Pez pelágico plateado con rayas onduladas azul verdosas en el dorso. Nada en grandes cardúmenes a alta velocidad. Es un recurso pesquero importante en Chile y todo el Pacífico.
species.description_en: Silvery pelagic fish with wavy blue-green stripes on the back. Swims in large schools at high speed. An important fishery resource in Chile and across the Pacific.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) FWC Fish and Wildlife Research Institute, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC-ND)

species.description_es: Pez enorme y poderoso con pico largo y aplanado como una espada. Puede nadar a más de 100 km/h. Es solitario y habita aguas oceánicas profundas, un trofeo de la pesca deportiva.
species.description_en: Enormous, powerful fish with a long, flat bill like a sword. Can swim over 100 km/h. Solitary, inhabiting deep oceanic waters. A sport fishing trophy.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Mariano Ordoñez, all rights reserved, uploaded by Mariano Ordoñez

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Ezequiel, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Gustavo Fernando Durán, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC-SA)
(c) miriamaguet, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Pez diminuto de agua dulce con manchas oscuras a lo largo del cuerpo. Las hembras son vivíparas y más grandes que los machos. Habita arroyos y lagunas costeras.
species.description_en: Tiny freshwater fish with dark spots along the body. Females are live-bearers and larger than males. Inhabits streams and coastal lagoons.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Ghislayne, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Ghislayne

species.description_es: Tunicado con exterior rugoso y pétreo pero interior rojo brillante. Se come crudo con limón en Chile y es un manjar de la gastronomía local. Filtra agua y se adhiere a rocas submarinas.
species.description_en: Tunicate with a rough, rock-like exterior but bright red interior. Eaten raw with lemon in Chile and is a local delicacy. Filter-feeds while attached to underwater rocks.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Felipe Campos, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Felipe Campos

species.description_es: Anémona grande con tentáculos rojo fuego y columna verde oliva cubierta de verrugas con arena. Es una de las anémonas más llamativas de Chile. Se encuentra en grietas rocosas poco profundas.
species.description_en: Large anemone with fiery red tentacles and an olive-green column covered with sand-studded warts. One of Chile's most striking anemones. Found in shallow rock crevices.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Lisa Bennett, some rights reserved (CC BY), uploaded by Lisa Bennett

species.description_es: Caracol pequeño de concha cónica negra azulada con interior nacarado. Se encuentra en rocas intermareales raspando algas. Común en las costas del sur de Chile.
species.description_en: Small snail with a bluish-black conical shell and pearly interior. Found on intertidal rocks grazing algae. Common on the coasts of southern Chile.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Tom Kennedy, some rights reserved (CC BY), uploaded by Tom Kennedy

species.description_es: Esponja masiva de color gris oscuro con forma de barril o domo. Puede alcanzar gran tamaño y vive en fondos rocosos. Es un filtrador que alberga pequeños organismos en sus canales internos.
species.description_en: Massive dark gray sponge shaped like a barrel or dome. Can grow very large and lives on rocky bottoms. A filter feeder that houses small organisms in its internal channels.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Karla Veloso Gallardo, all rights reserved, uploaded by Karla Veloso Gallardo

species.description_es: Mejillón pequeño de color verde oliva a café que forma densas colonias en el intermareal medio. Es hermafrodita, lo que le ayuda a colonizar rápidamente superficies nuevas.
species.description_en: Small olive-green to brown mussel forming dense colonies in the mid-intertidal zone. It is hermaphroditic, helping it quickly colonize new surfaces.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Jacob, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Jacob

species.description_es: Pez pelágico estilizado con rayas oblicuas oscuras en el dorso azulado. Nada en cardúmenes veloces cerca de la superficie. Muy apreciado en la pesca deportiva y como alimento.
species.description_en: Streamlined pelagic fish with dark oblique stripes on a blue back. Swims in fast schools near the surface. Highly valued in sport fishing and as food.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Gonzalo Bravo

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Nicolás Battini, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Nicolás Battini, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Anémona diminuta y colorida que parece una joya, con tentáculos de puntas esféricas brillantes. Forma colonias densas sobre paredes rocosas. Los colores van de naranja a rosa a púrpura.
species.description_en: Tiny, colorful anemone resembling a jewel, with brilliant ball-tipped tentacles. Forms dense colonies on rock walls. Colors range from orange to pink to purple.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Daniel Fernando Vidal, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Daniel Fernando Vidal

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Daniel Fernando Vidal, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Daniel Fernando Vidal, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Daniel Fernando Vidal, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Pez cartilaginoso con hocico alargado en forma de arado y color plateado con manchas oscuras. Usa su hocico para detectar presas enterradas en la arena. Pariente lejano de los tiburones.
species.description_en: Cartilaginous fish with an elongated plow-shaped snout and silvery color with dark spots. Uses its snout to detect prey buried in sand. A distant relative of sharks.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Erasmo Macaya Horta, some rights reserved (CC BY), uploaded by Erasmo Macaya Horta

species.description_es: Vieira con concha en forma de abanico de color púrpura a rosado con costillas radiales marcadas. Puede nadar abriendo y cerrando sus valvas. Se cultiva extensamente en Chile como marisco.
species.description_en: Scallop with a fan-shaped shell, purple to pink, with prominent radial ribs. Can swim by opening and closing its valves. Extensively farmed in Chile as seafood.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) dschr, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by dschr

species.description_es: Lapa pequeña y delicada con concha oval de color café con rayas claras. Se encuentra en la zona intermareal sobre rocas lisas. Tiene un patrón de desplazamiento de ida y vuelta a su sitio.
species.description_en: Small, delicate limpet with an oval brown shell with light stripes. Found in the intertidal zone on smooth rocks. Has a homing behavior, returning to the same spot.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Victor Molina, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Victor Molina

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Daniel Fernando Vidal, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Daniel Fernando Vidal, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Pez pequeñito y alargado de color café con rayas oscuras y aletas rojas. Se posa sobre las rocas con sus aletas pectorales. Muy territorial, defiende su pequeño territorio agresivamente.
species.description_en: Tiny elongated fish, brown with dark stripes and red fins. Perches on rocks using its pectoral fins. Very territorial, aggressively defending its small territory.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Rino, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Rino

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Rino, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Rino, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Rino, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Lapa con orificio apical ovalado y concha decorada con rayas radiales alternas claras y oscuras. Es de las fisurelas más decorativas de Chile. Habita el intermareal bajo.
species.description_en: Limpet with an oval apical hole and shell decorated with alternating light and dark radial stripes. One of Chile's most decorative keyhole limpets. Inhabits the low intertidal.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) karen Paz, all rights reserved, uploaded by karen Paz

species.description_es: Almeja de playa grande y ovalada de color blanco cremoso. Vive enterrada en la arena de playas expuestas al oleaje. Es la famosa 'macha' chilena, un marisco muy apreciado.
species.description_en: Large, oval beach clam with a creamy white shell. Lives buried in the sand of wave-exposed beaches. The famous Chilean 'macha', a highly prized shellfish.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) mattward,  זכויות יוצרים חלקיות (CC BY-NC), הועלה על ידי mattward

species.description_es: Alga verde con forma de tubos huecos comprimidos, de color verde brillante. Crece en pozas intermareales y zonas con agua dulce. Indica alta concentración de nutrientes en el agua.
species.description_en: Green alga shaped like compressed hollow tubes, bright green. Grows in tide pools and areas with freshwater input. Indicates high nutrient concentration in the water.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Bob Fuhrer, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Bob Fuhrer

species.description_es: Calamar gigante rojo que puede medir más de 1 metro y cambia de color rápidamente. Caza en grupos agresivos en aguas profundas y sube a la superficie de noche. También conocido como jibia.
species.description_en: Giant red squid that can exceed 1 meter and changes color rapidly. Hunts in aggressive groups in deep waters, rising to the surface at night. Also known as jumbo squid.

primary_photo.attribution: no rights reserved, uploaded by Zoltán Stekkelpak

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Cecilia Paula Alonso, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Cecilia Paula Alonso, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Lapa pequeña con orificio apical y concha con bandas alternas claras y oscuras. Es una de las fisurelas más pequeñas de Chile. Habita rocas intermareales y sublitorales someras.
species.description_en: Small limpet with an apical hole and shell with alternating light and dark bands. One of Chile's smallest keyhole limpets. Inhabits intertidal and shallow subtidal rocks.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Fabrice Prugnaud, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Fabrice Prugnaud

species.description_es: Caracol grande de concha globosa con cuadrícula de líneas marrones sobre fondo crema. Habita fondos arenosos donde se alimenta de erizos de mar. Su concha es muy decorativa.
species.description_en: Large snail with a globose shell featuring a grid of brown lines on a cream background. Inhabits sandy bottoms where it feeds on sea urchins. Its shell is very decorative.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Diego Almendras G., some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Diego Almendras G.

species.description_es: La fisurela más grande de Chile, con concha gruesa y ovalada que puede superar los 12 cm. Color café verdoso con orificio apical grande. Es comestible y se recolecta artesanalmente.
species.description_en: Chile's largest keyhole limpet, with a thick oval shell exceeding 12 cm. Greenish-brown with a large apical opening. Edible and harvested by artisanal fishers.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Sara Thiebaud, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Sara Thiebaud

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Mica Fraire, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Mica Fraire, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Mica Fraire, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Calamar de tamaño mediano de color rosado translúcido con manchas cromáticas. Habita aguas costeras en grandes cardúmenes. Es un recurso pesquero importante en la Patagonia.
species.description_en: Medium-sized squid, translucent pink with chromatic spots. Inhabits coastal waters in large schools. An important fishery resource in Patagonia.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Marcelo Andrés Rojas González, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Marcelo Andrés Rojas González

species.description_es: Cangrejo ovalado con caparazón convexo y liso de color café rosado. Se entierra parcialmente en arena. Sus pinzas son pequeñas comparadas con su cuerpo redondeado, y puede dar pellizcos dolorosos.
species.description_en: Oval crab with a convex, smooth pinkish-brown carapace. Partially buries in sand. Its claws are small compared to its rounded body, and it can give painful pinches.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Erasmo Macaya Horta, some rights reserved (CC BY), uploaded by Erasmo Macaya Horta

species.description_es: Cangrejito porcelana de color café con manchas blancas que vive como comensal en anémonas grandes. Filtra alimento del agua con sus maxilípedos plumosos. Inmune al veneno de su anémona hospedera.
species.description_en: Porcelain crab, brown with white spots, living as a commensal in large anemones. Filter-feeds from water with its feathery maxillipeds. Immune to its host anemone's venom.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Francisco Riquelme Tapia, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Francisco Riquelme Tapia

species.description_es: Cangrejo de caparazón redondo y convexo de color arena claro, perfectamente camuflado en fondos arenosos. Tiene patas largas y pinzas pequeñas. Se entierra en la arena dejando solo los ojos afuera.
species.description_en: Crab with a round, convex sand-colored carapace, perfectly camouflaged on sandy bottoms. Has long legs and small claws. Buries in sand leaving only its eyes exposed.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) antonia_lepe, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Jaiba grande y robusta de color rojo marmolado con pinzas masivas. Es la segunda jaiba más apreciada en Chile después de la jaiba mora. Habita fondos rocosos y arenosos.
species.description_en: Large, robust crab with mottled red coloring and massive claws. The second most prized crab in Chile after the purple crab. Inhabits rocky and sandy bottoms.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) jocean, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by jocean

species.description_es: Cangrejito diminuto de color gris plomo que vive como comensal dentro de bivalvos grandes. Tiene caparazón blando y redondeado. Se beneficia del alimento filtrado por su hospedero.
species.description_en: Tiny lead-gray crab that lives as a commensal inside large bivalves. Has a soft, rounded carapace. Benefits from food filtered by its host.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Fernando Gracia, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Fernando Gracia

species.description_es: Cangrejo araña pequeño de color café verdoso cubierto de algas e hidroides para camuflarse. Tiene patas largas y delgadas. Habita entre algas y bajo piedras en fondos rocosos.
species.description_en: Small spider crab, greenish-brown, covered with algae and hydroids for camouflage. Has long, thin legs. Lives among algae and under rocks on rocky bottoms.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) cstobie, some rights reserved (CC BY), uploaded by cstobie

species.description_es: Pez plateado pequeño y alargado que forma cardúmenes masivos de millones de individuos. Es la base de la cadena alimentaria marina y el recurso pesquero más importante de Chile y Perú.
species.description_en: Small, elongated silvery fish forming massive schools of millions. The foundation of the marine food chain and the most important fishery resource of Chile and Peru.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Mariano Rodriguez, all rights reserved, uploaded by Mariano Rodriguez

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Pablo Fraire, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Pablo Fraire, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Leonel Roget, some rights reserved (CC BY)

species.description_es: Pez robusto de color plateado verdoso con boca grande. Se encuentra en aguas costeras poco profundas, estuarios y ríos. Es muy apreciado en la pesca deportiva del sur de Chile.
species.description_en: Robust silvery-greenish fish with a large mouth. Found in shallow coastal waters, estuaries, and rivers. Highly valued in sport fishing in southern Chile.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) matias saa, all rights reserved, uploaded by matias saa

species.description_es: Cangrejito pequeño de color rojo granate con caparazón cubierto de granulaciones que parecen perlas. Habita bajo piedras y en grietas de arrecifes rocosos poco profundos.
species.description_en: Small garnet-red crab with a carapace covered in pearl-like granulations. Lives under rocks and in crevices of shallow rocky reefs.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Marcelo Andrés Rojas González, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Marcelo Andrés Rojas González

species.description_es: Cangrejito porcelana que vive entre los tentáculos de anémonas grandes, protegido de depredadores. Color café a gris con pinzas espinosas. Uno de los mejores ejemplos de comensalismo en Chile.
species.description_en: Porcelain crab living among tentacles of large anemones, protected from predators. Brown to gray with spiny claws. One of the best examples of commensalism in Chile.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Daniel Stange, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Daniel Stange

species.description_es: Blenio rechoncho de color verdoso a café con ojos grandes y cirros sobre la cabeza. Vive en agujeros de rocas y tubos vacíos de los que asoma la cabeza. Muy territorial.
species.description_en: Chunky blenny, greenish to brown with large eyes and cirri on the head. Lives in rock holes and empty tubes, poking its head out. Very territorial.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Leonardo Mondaca Lara, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Leonardo Mondaca Lara

species.description_es: Pez alargado de color café rosado que descansa sobre fondos arenosos cerca de arrecifes. Tiene boca pequeña y ojos grandes. Se alimenta de pequeños crustáceos y gusanos del fondo.
species.description_en: Elongated pinkish-brown fish that rests on sandy bottoms near reefs. Has a small mouth and large eyes. Feeds on small crustaceans and bottom-dwelling worms.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Jonatan Ferrada, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Jonatan Ferrada

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Daniel Fernando Vidal, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Daniel Fernando Vidal, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Daniel Fernando Vidal, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Nudibranquio aplanado de color blanco amarillento con manchas marrones. Tiene una textura granular en el dorso y branquias plumosas. Se alimenta de esponjas en arrecifes rocosos.
species.description_en: Flattened nudibranch, yellowish-white with brown spots. Has a granular texture on its back and feathery gills. Feeds on sponges on rocky reefs.
