primary_photo.attribution: (c) Francisco Riquelme Tapia, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Francisco Riquelme Tapia

species.description_es: Langosta achaparrada de color rojo intenso con pinzas largas y ojos grandes. Forma enjambres masivos en el fondo marino. Es un crustáceo importante en la cadena trófica del Pacífico sur.
species.description_en: Bright red squat lobster with long claws and large eyes. Forms massive swarms on the seafloor. An important crustacean in the South Pacific food chain.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) MatiasG, some rights reserved (CC BY-ND), uploaded by MatiasG

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY)
(c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY)
(c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY)

species.description_es: Nudibranquio pálido y translúcido con ceratas dorsales cortas. Difícil de ver por su coloración críptica. Se alimenta de hidroides en paredes rocosas y pilotes submarinos.
species.description_en: Pale, translucent nudibranch with short dorsal cerata. Hard to spot due to its cryptic coloration. Feeds on hydroids on rock walls and underwater pilings.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Gonzalo Bravo

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Gail Ashton PhD, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Estrella de mar con múltiples brazos (8-13) de color naranja a rojo brillante. Tiene una textura suave y aterciopelada. Habita fondos profundos y es un depredador de otros equinodermos.
species.description_en: Multi-armed sea star (8-13 arms), bright orange to red. Has a soft, velvety texture. Inhabits deep bottoms and preys on other echinoderms.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Matías Faúndez, all rights reserved, uploaded by Matías Faúndez

additional_photos.attribution: (c) ianwalker, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Nudibranquio amarillo limón uniforme sin manchas. Tiene una textura suave y cuerpo ovalado con branquias plumosas. Se alimenta de esponjas y se encuentra bajo piedras.
species.description_en: Uniform lemon-yellow nudibranch without spots. Has a smooth texture and oval body with feathery gills. Feeds on sponges and is found under rocks.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Marcelo Andrés Rojas González, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Marcelo Andrés Rojas González

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Kim Nesbitt, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Kim Nesbitt, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Kim Nesbitt, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Nudibranquio blanco con manchas negras como un dálmata y proyecciones naranjas en la cabeza. Uno de los nudibranquios más fotogénicos de Chile. Nombrado en honor a Charles Darwin.
species.description_en: White nudibranch with black dalmatian-like spots and orange projections on the head. One of Chile's most photogenic nudibranchs. Named after Charles Darwin.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) ulrich zanabria, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by ulrich zanabria

species.description_es: Anémona grande de color verde oscuro a marrón con tentáculos gruesos y cortos. Se encuentra firmemente adherida a rocas en zonas de oleaje fuerte. Es muy resistente a la desecación.
species.description_en: Large dark green to brown anemone with thick, short tentacles. Firmly attached to rocks in areas of strong wave action. Very resistant to desiccation.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Constanza Vergara V., some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Constanza Vergara V.

species.description_es: Quitón de tamaño mediano con placas de colores variables — verdes, marrones o rosadas. Cinturón estrecho con escamas finas. Común en el intermareal rocoso de Chile central.
species.description_en: Medium-sized chiton with variable-colored plates — green, brown, or pink. Narrow girdle with fine scales. Common on the rocky intertidal of central Chile.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Juan Francisco Araya, Marta Esther Araya, some rights reserved (CC BY)

species.description_es: Quitón con placas cubiertas de pequeñas espinas que le dan un aspecto erizado. Color café oscuro a negro. Se adhiere a rocas en la zona intermareal baja de Chile central.
species.description_en: Chiton with plates covered in small spines giving it a bristly appearance. Dark brown to black. Clings to rocks in the low intertidal zone of central Chile.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Diego Gutierrez, all rights reserved, uploaded by Diego Gutierrez

species.description_es: Cangrejito porcelana de color gris con caparazón granuloso y pinzas aplanadas. Se encuentra bajo piedras en el intermareal filtrando alimento del agua. Muy común pero discreto.
species.description_en: Gray porcelain crab with a granular carapace and flattened claws. Found under rocks in the intertidal zone, filter-feeding from the water. Very common but discreet.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Heather G., all rights reserved, uploaded by Heather G.

additional_photos.attribution: no rights reserved
(c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Cangrejo araña con rostro largo y puntiagudo y patas extremadamente largas y delgadas. Color café claro. Se camufla con algas y otros organismos que crecen sobre su caparazón.
species.description_en: Spider crab with a long, pointed rostrum and extremely long, thin legs. Light brown. Camouflages with algae and other organisms growing on its carapace.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Benjamín Cortés Fuentes, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Benjamín Cortés Fuentes

additional_photos.attribution: no rights reserved
(c) Alex Lamoreaux, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Rony Ristow, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Cirripedio de concha blanca cónica y lisa que se adhiere a rocas, conchas y estructuras costeras. Forma costras densas en el intermareal bajo. Filtra alimento extendiendo sus cirros plumosos.
species.description_en: White conical smooth-shelled barnacle that attaches to rocks, shells, and coastal structures. Forms dense crusts in the low intertidal. Filter-feeds by extending feathery cirri.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Claudio Maureira, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC-SA), uploaded by Claudio Maureira

species.description_es: Caracol con concha cónica baja que parece un sombrero chino. Se apila sobre otros individuos. Tiene una plataforma interna que cubre la mitad de la abertura. Común en rocas sublitorales.
species.description_en: Snail with a low conical shell resembling a Chinese hat. Stacks on top of other individuals. Has an internal shelf covering half the opening. Common on subtidal rocks.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Tami Morales, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Tami Morales

species.description_es: Lapa pequeña con concha oval baja y costillas finas, de color gris a café. Habita rocas intermareales del centro-sur de Chile. Se desplaza por la roca raspando microalgas.
species.description_en: Small limpet with a low oval shell and fine ribs, gray to brown. Inhabits intertidal rocks of central-southern Chile. Moves across rock scraping microalgae.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Bastian Riveros Flores, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Bastian Riveros Flores

species.description_es: Lapa con concha verdosa aplanada y textura lisa. Se encuentra en la zona intermareal media adherida firmemente a las rocas. Común en toda la costa de Chile central.
species.description_en: Limpet with a flat greenish shell and smooth texture. Found in the mid-intertidal zone firmly attached to rocks. Common throughout the central Chilean coast.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Gonzalo Bravo

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Cirripedio diminuto y gris que forma costras densas en la zona intermareal alta. Su superficie es rugosa y áspera. Es una de las primeras especies que coloniza la roca expuesta.
species.description_en: Tiny gray barnacle forming dense crusts in the upper intertidal zone. Its surface is rough and coarse. One of the first species to colonize exposed rock.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) camila-rivera, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by camila-rivera

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Pete Woodall, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Pete Woodall, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Alga roja comestible con láminas delgadas y translúcidas de color rojo púrpura. Conocida como luche, es un ingrediente tradicional de la cocina chilena. Crece en la zona intermareal alta.
species.description_en: Edible red alga with thin, translucent red-purple blades. Known as 'luche', it's a traditional ingredient in Chilean cuisine. Grows in the upper intertidal zone.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Claudio Maureira, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC-SA), uploaded by Claudio Maureira

species.description_es: Caracolito diminuto de color gris con forma cónica que vive en la zona supralitoral. Sobrevive largos periodos fuera del agua adherido a las rocas. Se agrupa en grietas durante la marea baja.
species.description_en: Tiny gray conical snail living in the supralittoral zone. Survives long periods out of water clinging to rocks. Clusters in crevices during low tide.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) giancarlo muñoz, all rights reserved, uploaded by giancarlo muñoz

species.description_es: Lapa pequeña con concha cónica baja decorada con líneas radiales finas. Color variable de gris a marrón. Común en rocas intermareales de Chile central, pastoreando microalgas.
species.description_en: Small limpet with a low conical shell decorated with fine radial lines. Variable color from gray to brown. Common on intertidal rocks of central Chile, grazing microalgae.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Bastian Riveros Flores, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Bastian Riveros Flores

species.description_es: Lapa intermareal de concha muy variable en forma y color — desde café liso hasta gris con costillas. Su nombre refleja esta variabilidad. Común en toda la costa de Chile.
species.description_en: Intertidal limpet with a highly variable shell in shape and color — from smooth brown to ribbed gray. Its name reflects this variability. Common along all of Chile's coast.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) nicocraker, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Pez robusto de color café con barras verticales oscuras y labios gruesos. Tiene aletas pectorales con rayos libres que usa para 'caminar' por el fondo. Muy común en arrecifes rocosos.
species.description_en: Robust brown fish with dark vertical bars and thick lips. Has pectoral fins with free rays used to 'walk' along the bottom. Very common on rocky reefs.

primary_photo.attribution:
(c) 

Manuel CABALLER, some rights reserved (CC BY)

species.description_es: Caracol grande de concha gruesa y oscura, de color chocolate. Es un depredador de cirripedios y mejillones. Conocido como 'locate', es comestible y se recolecta artesanalmente.
species.description_en: Large snail with a thick, dark chocolate-colored shell. A predator of barnacles and mussels. Known as 'locate', it's edible and harvested by artisanal fishers.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Leslie Harris, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Leslie Harris

species.description_es: Caracol elegante con concha lisa y brillante en tonos oliva y café. Se desplaza sobre fondos arenosos donde caza pequeños invertebrados. Su concha pulida es muy atractiva para coleccionistas.
species.description_en: Elegant snail with a smooth, glossy olive and brown shell. Moves across sandy bottoms hunting small invertebrates. Its polished shell is highly attractive to collectors.

primary_photo.attribution: (с) biodiversidad _de_chile, все права защищены, загрузил biodiversidad _de_chile

species.description_es: Pez aplanado de cabeza grande con piel verrugosa y manchas oscuras. Se esconde enterrado en arena o bajo piedras. Produce sonidos gruñendo y puede dar una picadura dolorosa con sus espinas.
species.description_en: Flat-headed fish with warty skin and dark spots. Hides buried in sand or under rocks. Produces grunting sounds and can deliver a painful sting with its spines.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) jgrimshaw, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Pez de aguas profundas de color azul oscuro a negro con hocico puntiagudo y ojos grandes. Vive en fondos batiales a cientos de metros de profundidad. Rara vez visto por buzos.
species.description_en: Deep-water fish, dark blue to black with a pointed snout and large eyes. Lives on bathyal bottoms at hundreds of meters depth. Rarely seen by divers.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) nicocraker, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Blenio grande de color verdoso oscuro a negro con labios gruesos y expresión seria. Es el blenio más grande de Chile. Muy territorial, defiende su refugio agresivamente contra intrusos.
species.description_en: Large blenny, dark greenish to black with thick lips and a serious expression. Chile's largest blenny. Very territorial, aggressively defending its shelter against intruders.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Claudio Maureira, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC-SA), uploaded by Claudio Maureira

species.description_es: Pez alargado y plateado con boca grande armada de dientes afilados. Es la merluza nativa de Chile y uno de los recursos pesqueros más importantes. Habita fondos de la plataforma continental.
species.description_en: Elongated silvery fish with a large mouth armed with sharp teeth. Chile's native hake and one of the most important fishery resources. Inhabits continental shelf bottoms.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) cstobie, some rights reserved (CC BY), uploaded by cstobie

species.description_es: Pez robusto de color gris verdoso con cuerpo ovalado y escamas gruesas. Se alimenta de algas en arrecifes rocosos poco profundos. Es curioso con los buzos y fácil de observar.
species.description_en: Robust greenish-gray fish with an oval body and thick scales. Feeds on algae in shallow rocky reefs. Curious around divers and easy to observe.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Hectonichus, some rights reserved (CC BY-SA)

species.description_es: Caracol con concha ornamentada con espinas frondosas y varices prominentes. Color café oscuro. Depredador de bivalvos y otros moluscos que perfora con su rádula.
species.description_en: Snail with an ornate shell featuring frondose spines and prominent varices. Dark brown. A predator of bivalves and other mollusks, drilling through shells with its radula.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Sebas Cavour Astudillo Araya, all rights reserved, uploaded by Sebas Cavour Astudillo Araya

species.description_es: Estrella de mar con brazos largos y delgados de color gris azulado. Se desliza rápidamente sobre fondos arenosos cazando otros equinodermos. Puede autotomizar sus brazos para escapar.
species.description_en: Sea star with long, slender grayish-blue arms. Glides quickly across sandy bottoms hunting other echinoderms. Can shed its arms to escape predators.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Marcelo Andrés Rojas González, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Marcelo Andrés Rojas González

species.description_es: Blenio verde oscuro con manchas claras y cabeza grande. Similar al borracho gigante pero más pequeño. Habita grietas rocosas y pozas intermareales donde se alimenta de algas.
species.description_en: Dark green blenny with light spots and a large head. Similar to the giant blenny but smaller. Inhabits rocky crevices and tide pools where it feeds on algae.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Marcelo Andrés Rojas González, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Marcelo Andrés Rojas González

species.description_es: Blenio verdoso con manchas oscuras y cuerpo alargado. Vive en las pozas de marea donde pasta algas sobre las rocas. Es herbívoro y muy común en la zona intermareal de Chile central.
species.description_en: Greenish blenny with dark spots and an elongated body. Lives in tide pools where it grazes algae on rocks. Herbivorous and very common in central Chile's intertidal zone.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Clinton Duffy, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Clinton Duffy

species.description_es: Pez pelágico plateado con línea lateral curvada y escudetes óseos. Forma enormes cardúmenes en aguas costeras. Es el recurso pesquero más importante de Chile por volumen de captura.
species.description_en: Silvery pelagic fish with a curved lateral line and bony scutes. Forms enormous schools in coastal waters. Chile's most important fishery resource by catch volume.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Vuelve al Océano, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Vuelve al Océano

species.description_es: Pez pipa delgado y alargado con cuerpo segmentado como una ramita. Color café a verde para camuflarse entre las algas. Los machos incuban los huevos en una bolsa ventral, como los caballitos de mar.
species.description_en: Slender, elongated pipefish with a segmented body like a twig. Brown to green for camouflage among algae. Males incubate eggs in a ventral pouch, like seahorses.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Brs.Cisternas, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Brs.Cisternas

species.description_es: Pez de cuerpo comprimido de color verde azulado con puntos claros. Se alimenta exclusivamente de algas en arrecifes rocosos. Es uno de los peces más emblemáticos del buceo en Chile central.
species.description_en: Compressed-bodied fish, blue-green with light spots. Feeds exclusively on algae on rocky reefs. One of the most emblematic fish for diving in central Chile.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) gaston-fernandez, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Pez alargado y plateado con una franja lateral brillante. Nada en cardúmenes cerca de la superficie en aguas costeras. Es popular como alimento y se vende fresco en los mercados chilenos.
species.description_en: Elongated silvery fish with a bright lateral stripe. Swims in schools near the surface in coastal waters. Popular as food and sold fresh in Chilean markets.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Gonzalo Bravo

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Mauro Marcinkevicius, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Pez pequeño y acorazado con cuerpo cubierto de placas óseas. Color café con manchas claras. Vive en fondos arenosos y rocosos donde se desplaza lentamente buscando pequeños invertebrados.
species.description_en: Small armored fish with a body covered in bony plates. Brown with light spots. Lives on sandy and rocky bottoms, slowly searching for small invertebrates.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Oschmach, some rights reserved (CC BY-SA)

species.description_es: Pez plateado con cuerpo ovalado comprimido y rayas horizontales doradas. Forma cardúmenes en arrecifes rocosos y es muy común en la zona central. Apreciado en la gastronomía local.
species.description_en: Silvery fish with a compressed oval body and golden horizontal stripes. Schools on rocky reefs and is very common in the central zone. Valued in local gastronomy.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) jorgelaras, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Pez largo y serpentiforme de color rojo rosado, considerado el más fino de Chile para comer. Vive en cuevas y grietas rocosas profundas. Su carne blanca y firme es exquisita.
species.description_en: Long, serpentine fish, pinkish-red, considered Chile's finest eating fish. Lives in deep rocky caves and crevices. Its white, firm flesh is exquisite.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Abel Romero, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Abel Romero

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Abel Romero, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Abel Romero, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Abel Romero, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Pez extraño con cabeza grande y hocico alargado que apunta hacia abajo. Color café rosado con aspecto prehistórico. Camina por el fondo usando sus aletas pectorales modificadas.
species.description_en: Strange fish with a large head and elongated downward-pointing snout. Pinkish-brown with a prehistoric appearance. Walks along the bottom using modified pectoral fins.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Paula González Valderrama, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Paula González Valderrama

species.description_es: Pez pequeño y rechoncho de color café moteado con cabeza grande y espinas prominentes. Se posa sobre las rocas con sus aletas pectorales. Común en pozas intermareales de Chile central.
species.description_en: Small, chunky mottled brown fish with a large head and prominent spines. Perches on rocks with its pectoral fins. Common in tide pools of central Chile.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Mariano Rodriguez, all rights reserved, uploaded by Mariano Rodriguez

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Ofiuro espectacular con brazos que se ramifican repetidamente como un árbol. Color naranja a rojo. Extiende sus brazos como una red para capturar plancton. Uno de los invertebrados más impresionantes.
species.description_en: Spectacular basket star with arms that branch repeatedly like a tree. Orange to red. Extends its arms like a net to capture plankton. One of the most impressive invertebrates.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) jorgelaras, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

additional_photos.attribution: no rights reserved

species.description_es: Cangrejo pequeño con franjas alternas claras y oscuras en las patas. Tiene pinzas fuertes que usa en peleas territoriales. Habita grietas y oquedades en rocas intermareales.
species.description_en: Small crab with alternating light and dark bands on the legs. Has strong claws used in territorial fights. Inhabits crevices and hollows in intertidal rocks.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) djhiker, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by djhiker

species.description_es: Cangrejo violinista con una pinza enormemente agrandada que el macho agita para atraer hembras. La otra pinza es diminuta. Vive en madrigueras en manglares y zonas fangosas.
species.description_en: Fiddler crab with one enormously enlarged claw that the male waves to attract females. The other claw is tiny. Lives in burrows in mangroves and muddy areas.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) MatiasG, some rights reserved (CC BY-ND), uploaded by MatiasG

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Camarón pequeño y transparente con franjas rojas. Vive en pozas de marea y entre algas en la zona intermareal. Sale de noche a buscar alimento y es presa de muchos peces costeros.
species.description_en: Small, transparent shrimp with red stripes. Lives in tide pools and among algae in the intertidal zone. Forages at night and is prey for many coastal fish.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) ulrich zanabria, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by ulrich zanabria

species.description_es: Crustáceo excavador de aspecto primitivo con caparazón espinoso y antenas largas. Vive enterrado en arena de playas expuestas al oleaje. Se mueve rápidamente cavando hacia atrás.
species.description_en: Primitive-looking burrowing crustacean with a spiny carapace and long antennae. Lives buried in sand on wave-exposed beaches. Moves quickly by digging backward.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Diego Almendras G., some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Diego Almendras G.

species.description_es: Raya pequeña de fondo con piel áspera cubierta de espinas diminutas como una lija. Color café con manchas oscuras. Se entierra en arena donde acecha invertebrados y peces pequeños.
species.description_en: Small bottom-dwelling ray with rough skin covered in tiny thorns like sandpaper. Brown with dark spots. Buries in sand where it ambushes invertebrates and small fish.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Steve Smith, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC-ND), uploaded by Steve Smith

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Romi Galeota Lencina, some rights reserved (CC BY)
(c) Romi Galeota Lencina, some rights reserved (CC BY)
(c) Romi Galeota Lencina, some rights reserved (CC BY)

species.description_es: Nudibranquio eólido con ceratas dorsales marrones a naranja y cuerpo translúcido. Se alimenta de anémonas y almacena sus células urticantes para defensa propia. Común en aguas temperadas.
species.description_en: Aeolid nudibranch with brown to orange dorsal cerata and a translucent body. Feeds on anemones and stores their stinging cells for its own defense. Common in temperate waters.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Ignacio Contreras, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Ignacio Contreras

species.description_es: Tiburón pequeño de color café grisáceo sin marcas llamativas. Habita fondos profundos de la plataforma continental. Es muy lento y de comportamiento pasivo, completamente inofensivo.
species.description_en: Small grayish-brown shark without striking markings. Inhabits deep continental shelf bottoms. Very slow and passive in behavior, completely harmless.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) james_holkko, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Alga roja delicada con ramificaciones alternas en forma de peine, de color rojo intenso a rosado. Forma matas decorativas sobre rocas sublitorales. Una de las algas rojas más bonitas.
species.description_en: Delicate red alga with alternate comb-like branching, deep red to pink. Forms decorative tufts on subtidal rocks. One of the most beautiful red algae.

primary_photo.attribution:
(c) 

Gargominy O., some rights reserved (CC BY)

species.description_es: Caracol marino de concha gruesa con labio externo engrosado. Color gris a café. Se alimenta de cirripedios perforando sus conchas. Común en el intermareal rocoso de Chile central.
species.description_en: Marine snail with a thick shell and thickened outer lip. Gray to brown. Feeds on barnacles by drilling their shells. Common on the rocky intertidal of central Chile.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Nicolas Olejnik, some rights reserved (CC BY), uploaded by Nicolas Olejnik

additional_photos.attribution: (c) larsonek, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Agustín Gabriel Álvarez, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Agustín Gabriel Álvarez, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Lapa grande con concha cónica lisa de color café dorado con interior nacarado brillante. Habita las costas del extremo sur de Chile y es importante en la gastronomía mapuche.
species.description_en: Large limpet with a smooth golden-brown conical shell and brilliant pearly interior. Inhabits the coasts of extreme southern Chile and is important in Mapuche gastronomy.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Charcos Companhia, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Charcos Companhia

additional_photos.attribution: (c) rodriheliz, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) rodriheliz, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Lucas Rubio, some rights reserved (CC BY)

species.description_es: Caracol con concha aplanada tipo zapatilla que se adhiere a rocas y conchas. Tiene una plataforma interna blanca. Las hembras incuban sus huevos dentro de la concha hasta que eclosionan.
species.description_en: Snail with a flat slipper-like shell that attaches to rocks and shells. Has a white internal shelf. Females brood eggs inside the shell until they hatch.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Richard Taylor, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Richard Taylor

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Julien Renoult, some rights reserved (CC BY)
(c) Julien Renoult, some rights reserved (CC BY)
(c) Enrique Montes, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Alga parda pequeña con forma de bolsita o utrículo inflado de aire. Color café oscuro. Crece en racimos en pozas intermareales y rocas expuestas al oleaje.
species.description_en: Small brown alga shaped like a little air-inflated bag or utricle. Dark brown. Grows in clusters in tide pools and wave-exposed rocks.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Erasmo Macaya Horta, some rights reserved (CC BY), uploaded by Erasmo Macaya Horta

species.description_es: Alga parda con frondas dicotómicas aplanadas de color café oliváceo con reflejos iridiscentes bajo el agua. Forma matas densas en arrecifes rocosos poco profundos.
species.description_en: Brown alga with flat dichotomous fronds, olive-brown with iridescent reflections underwater. Forms dense tufts on shallow rocky reefs.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) pia_ampuero, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by pia_ampuero

species.description_es: Quitón pequeño con placas esculturadas y cinturón cubierto de cerdas cortas. Color variable de verde a marrón. Se encuentra en rocas intermareales del Pacífico sudamericano.
species.description_en: Small chiton with sculptured plates and a girdle covered in short bristles. Variable color from green to brown. Found on intertidal rocks of the South American Pacific.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Petro, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Petro

species.description_es: Alga roja costrosa que forma manchas rosadas a rojas planas sobre las rocas, como pintura. Crece pegada a la roca y es muy difícil de desprender. Indicadora de costas rocosas estables.
species.description_en: Crustose red alga forming flat pink to red patches on rocks, like paint. Grows flush against rock and is very hard to remove. Indicates stable rocky shores.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Cecilia Orellana Muñoz, all rights reserved, uploaded by Cecilia Orellana Muñoz

species.description_es: Caracol de concha turbinada negra brillante con interior nacarado. Se adhiere a rocas intermareales donde raspa algas. Su concha gruesa lo protege del oleaje fuerte.
species.description_en: Snail with a shiny black turbinate shell and pearly interior. Clings to intertidal rocks where it grazes algae. Its thick shell protects it from strong waves.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) rdelpiano, all rights reserved, uploaded by rdelpiano

species.description_es: Caracol con concha alargada y en espiral con costillas espirales marcadas. Color café claro. Habita fondos arenosos y fangosos donde se alimenta de microalgas y detritus.
species.description_en: Snail with an elongated spiral shell with prominent spiral ribs. Light brown. Inhabits sandy and muddy bottoms, feeding on microalgae and detritus.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) rickt,  זכויות יוצרים חלקיות (CC BY-NC), הועלה על ידי rickt

species.description_es: Alga parda con forma de cinta aplanada, de color café oliváceo. Aparece estacionalmente en pozas de marea y rocas del intermareal bajo. Es efímera y crece rápidamente en primavera.
species.description_en: Brown alga shaped like a flat ribbon, olive-brown. Appears seasonally in tide pools and low intertidal rocks. Ephemeral and grows quickly in spring.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Felipe I. Torres, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Felipe I. Torres

species.description_es: Lapa con concha gruesa decorada con costillas radiales prominentes y orificio apical. Color gris rosado. Habita rocas intermareales y sublitorales someras de Chile central.
species.description_en: Limpet with a thick shell decorated with prominent radial ribs and an apical opening. Pinkish-gray. Inhabits intertidal and shallow subtidal rocks of central Chile.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Marcelo Andrés Rojas González, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Marcelo Andrés Rojas González

species.description_es: Fisurela grande con concha ovalada y margen ancho. Color café verdoso con orificio apical alargado. Es una de las lapas más grandes y es cosechada como alimento.
species.description_en: Large keyhole limpet with an oval shell and wide margin. Greenish-brown with an elongated apical opening. One of the largest limpets, harvested as food.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) pia_ampuero, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by pia_ampuero

species.description_es: Lapa de concha oscura casi negra con orificio apical. Textura relativamente lisa comparada con otras fisurelas. Común en el intermareal bajo de rocas expuestas al oleaje.
species.description_en: Limpet with a nearly black shell and apical opening. Relatively smooth texture compared to other keyhole limpets. Common in the low intertidal on wave-exposed rocks.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Matías Berrocal, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Matías Berrocal

species.description_es: Cangrejito porcelana de color café liso con pinzas aplanadas. Muy común bajo piedras intermareales donde filtra alimento. Se confunde fácilmente con otros Petrolisthes.
species.description_en: Smooth brown porcelain crab with flattened claws. Very common under intertidal rocks where it filter-feeds. Easily confused with other Petrolisthes species.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) kevin1300d, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Cangrejo pequeño de colores vistosos con caparazón redondeado y pinzas fuertes. Habita bajo rocas y en grietas en la zona intermareal de Chile central. Es poco conocido pero llamativo.
species.description_en: Small colorful crab with a rounded carapace and strong claws. Lives under rocks and in crevices in the intertidal zone of central Chile. Little-known but striking.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) brhong,  זכויות יוצרים חלקיות (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Alga roja filamentosa con ramificaciones dicotómicas y nódulos visibles a lo largo de los filamentos. Color rojo oscuro. Forma matas pequeñas sobre rocas y otras algas.
species.description_en: Filamentous red alga with dichotomous branching and visible nodes along the filaments. Dark red. Forms small tufts on rocks and other algae.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) SofiRS, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by SofiRS

species.description_es: Cangrejo xántido pequeño de color café oscuro con pinzas robustas de puntas negras. Se esconde bajo piedras y en grietas. Tiene un comportamiento defensivo con las pinzas levantadas.
species.description_en: Small xanthid crab, dark brown with robust black-tipped claws. Hides under rocks and in crevices. Displays defensive behavior with claws raised.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) jonas-g, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by jonas-g

species.description_es: Cangrejito porcelana con caparazón anguloso de color café a gris. Vive bajo piedras y en grietas del intermareal, filtrando alimento con sus maxilípedos plumosos.
species.description_en: Porcelain crab with an angular brown to gray carapace. Lives under rocks and in intertidal crevices, filter-feeding with its feathery maxillipeds.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Nicolás Battini, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Nicolás Battini

additional_photos.attribution: (c) raulsstt, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) raulsstt, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Nicolas Olejnik, some rights reserved (CC BY)

species.description_es: Cangrejo araña pequeño con caparazón pentagonal cubierto de algas e hidroides para camuflaje. Se mueve lentamente entre las algas donde es casi invisible.
species.description_en: Small spider crab with a pentagonal carapace covered in algae and hydroids for camouflage. Moves slowly among algae where it is nearly invisible.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Mariano Rodriguez, all rights reserved, uploaded by Mariano Rodriguez

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Jonatan Ferrada, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Anémona de color rojo sangre con tentáculos cortos y gruesos. Se encuentra en paredes rocosas y bajo salientes en la zona sublitoral. Su intenso color rojo la hace inconfundible.
species.description_en: Blood-red anemone with short, thick tentacles. Found on rock walls and under overhangs in the subtidal zone. Its intense red color makes it unmistakable.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Jehieli Ocayo Ocaranza, all rights reserved, uploaded by Jehieli Ocayo Ocaranza

species.description_es: Mejillón pequeño con costillas radiales pronunciadas y color café oscuro. Forma bancos densos en el intermareal medio, generalmente por encima de los choritos maico.
species.description_en: Small mussel with pronounced radial ribs and dark brown color. Forms dense beds in the mid-intertidal, usually above the purple mussel zone.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Rob Westerduijn, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Rob Westerduijn

species.description_es: Lapa con concha de borde grueso y orificio apical, de color café verdoso. Tiene una textura finamente esculturada. Habita el intermareal bajo de rocas expuestas.
species.description_en: Limpet with a thick-rimmed shell and apical opening, greenish-brown. Has a finely sculptured texture. Inhabits the low intertidal of exposed rocks.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Mariano Rodriguez, all rights reserved, uploaded by Mariano Rodriguez

additional_photos.attribution: (c) avocat, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Alex Smilor, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Pablo, some rights reserved (CC BY)

species.description_es: Lapa de concha cónica dorada brillante con interior nacarado. Su hermoso color dorado la distingue de otras lapas. Habita costas rocosas del sur de Chile y Patagonia.
species.description_en: Limpet with a brilliant golden conical shell and pearly interior. Its beautiful golden color distinguishes it from other limpets. Inhabits rocky coasts of southern Chile and Patagonia.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) ulrich zanabria, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by ulrich zanabria

species.description_es: Cangrejo pequeño de caparazón espinoso de color café. Tiene pinzas fuertes y desiguales. Habita grietas y bajo piedras en el intermareal de Chile central.
species.description_en: Small crab with a spiny brown carapace. Has strong, unequal claws. Inhabits crevices and under rocks in the intertidal zone of central Chile.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Diego Almendras G., some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Diego Almendras G.

species.description_es: Cirripedio de concha blanca pequeña con forma de flor cuando abre sus placas operculares. Habita el intermareal bajo y zona sublitoral. Filtra plancton con sus cirros.
species.description_en: Small white-shelled barnacle that looks flower-like when it opens its opercular plates. Inhabits the low intertidal and subtidal zone. Filters plankton with its cirri.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) dr. Jackson W.F. Chu, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC-SA), uploaded by dr. Jackson W.F. Chu

species.description_es: Pepino de mar aplanado cubierto de escamas calcáreas que parecen tejas. Solo la cara ventral tiene pies ambulacrales. Habita fondos rocosos profundos y paredes verticales.
species.description_en: Flattened sea cucumber covered in calcareous scales resembling tiles. Only the ventral surface has tube feet. Inhabits deep rocky bottoms and vertical walls.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Marcelo Andrés Rojas González, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Marcelo Andrés Rojas González

species.description_es: Pez grande y robusto completamente negro con labios gruesos y dientes fuertes. Es muy territorial y curioso con los buzos. Se alimenta de erizos y moluscos que tritura con sus mandíbulas.
species.description_en: Large, robust all-black fish with thick lips and strong teeth. Very territorial and curious around divers. Feeds on sea urchins and mollusks, crushing them with its jaws.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Claude Nozères, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Claude Nozères

species.description_es: Pez de aguas profundas con cuerpo alargado y negro, mandíbula grande con dientes como agujas, y órganos luminosos a lo largo del cuerpo. Habita la zona mesopelágica a batipelágica.
species.description_en: Deep-water fish with an elongated black body, large jaw with needle-like teeth, and light organs along its body. Inhabits the mesopelagic to bathypelagic zone.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) helenpope, all rights reserved

species.description_es: Erizo de mar de caparazón grueso y abultado con espinas cortas y gruesas. Color verde oliva a marrón. Se encuentra en fondos rocosos raspando algas con su aparato masticador.
species.description_en: Sea urchin with a thick, humped test and short, thick spines. Olive-green to brown. Found on rocky bottoms grazing algae with its jaw apparatus.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) vichepatata, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Pez pequeño de color gris azulado con cola ahorquillada que forma cardúmenes sobre arrecifes rocosos. Es la única damisela del Pacífico temperado sudamericano. Endémico de Chile central.
species.description_en: Small blue-gray fish with a forked tail that schools over rocky reefs. The only damselfish of the temperate South American Pacific. Endemic to central Chile.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Paula González Valderrama, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Paula González Valderrama

species.description_es: Pez pequeño y aplanado con ventosa ventral poderosa. Color café marmolado para camuflarse con las rocas. Se adhiere tan fuerte a las rocas que es casi imposible despegarlo.
species.description_en: Small, flattened fish with a powerful ventral suction cup. Marbled brown for rock camouflage. Clings so strongly to rocks that it's almost impossible to detach.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) micahq, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by micahq

species.description_es: Pez cinta largo y plateado con cuerpo comprimido lateralmente y aleta dorsal roja. Vive en aguas profundas y solo aparece en superficie cuando está enfermo o desorientado. Un avistamiento poco común.
species.description_en: Long, silvery ribbonfish with a laterally compressed body and red dorsal fin. Lives in deep waters, only appearing at the surface when sick or disoriented. An uncommon sighting.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) jgrimshaw, einige Rechte vorbehalten (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Tiburón de aguas profundas con hocico extremadamente largo y aplanado como un pico de ave. Color gris oscuro. Habita el talud continental a grandes profundidades.
species.description_en: Deep-water shark with an extremely long, flat snout like a bird's beak. Dark gray. Inhabits the continental slope at great depths.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) ulrich zanabria, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by ulrich zanabria

species.description_es: Pez plateado grande con reflejos dorados y cuerpo comprimido. Se encuentra en aguas costeras y estuarios donde se alimenta de invertebrados bentónicos. Es una especie apreciada por pescadores.
species.description_en: Large silvery fish with golden reflections and a compressed body. Found in coastal waters and estuaries feeding on benthic invertebrates. Valued by fishermen.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Marcelo Andrés Rojas González, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Marcelo Andrés Rojas González

species.description_es: Pez pequeño y colorido con manchas blancas en las mejillas y cuerpo café con bandas. Habita grietas y bajo piedras en arrecifes rocosos. Las hembras tienen colores más apagados.
species.description_en: Small, colorful fish with white cheek spots and a banded brown body. Lives in crevices and under rocks on rocky reefs. Females have more muted colors.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Paula González Valderrama, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Paula González Valderrama

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Pollerspoeck Juergen, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Pollerspoeck Juergen, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Pollerspoeck Juergen, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Raya eléctrica pequeña de forma circular y color café uniforme. Produce descargas eléctricas para aturdir presas y defenderse. Vive en fondos arenosos cerca de la costa.
species.description_en: Small electric ray, circular in shape and uniform brown. Produces electric shocks to stun prey and defend itself. Lives on sandy bottoms near the coast.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) jorgelaras, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Quitón con placas esculturadas y cinturón cubierto de cerdas visibles. Color café verdoso. Endémico de Chile, se encuentra en rocas intermareales del centro y sur del país.
species.description_en: Chiton with sculptured plates and a girdle covered with visible bristles. Greenish-brown. Endemic to Chile, found on intertidal rocks of the central and southern coast.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Blythe Nilson, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Blythe Nilson

species.description_es: Raya torpedo grande y redondeada capaz de producir fuertes descargas eléctricas de hasta 220 voltios. Color café oscuro uniforme. Habita fondos arenosos y fangosos del Pacífico sudamericano.
species.description_en: Large, rounded torpedo ray capable of producing strong electric shocks up to 220 volts. Uniform dark brown. Inhabits sandy and muddy bottoms of the South American Pacific.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Daniel Stange, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Daniel Stange

species.description_es: Pez alargado de color verde brillante a esmeralda con manchas oscuras. Vive entre algas y kelp donde su color lo camufla perfectamente. Uno de los peces más coloridos de Chile.
species.description_en: Elongated fish, bright green to emerald with dark spots. Lives among algae and kelp where its color provides perfect camouflage. One of Chile's most colorful fish.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Diego Almendras G., some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Diego Almendras G.

species.description_es: Almeja de concha ovalada y gruesa de color blanco cremoso. Vive enterrada en fondos arenosos y fangosos. Es un marisco importante en la gastronomía del sur de Chile.
species.description_en: Clam with a thick oval shell, creamy white. Lives buried in sandy and muddy bottoms. An important shellfish in southern Chilean gastronomy.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Gonzalo Bravo

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Daniel Fernando Vidal, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Daniel Fernando Vidal, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Nudibranquio de color blanco a crema con papilas cortas y densas por todo el dorso que le dan aspecto áspero. Se alimenta de esponjas en arrecifes rocosos poco profundos.
species.description_en: White to cream nudibranch with short, dense papillae across its back giving it a rough appearance. Feeds on sponges in shallow rocky reefs.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Gonzalo Bravo

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Cecilia Paula Alonso, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Cecilia Paula Alonso, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Nudibranquio eólido con ceratas rojas a naranjas sobre cuerpo translúcido. Se alimenta de hidroides y es relativamente común en aguas frías del sur. Almacena células urticantes de sus presas.
species.description_en: Aeolid nudibranch with red to orange cerata on a translucent body. Feeds on hydroids and is relatively common in cold southern waters. Stores stinging cells from its prey.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Daniel, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Daniel

species.description_es: Anguila serpentiforme con cuerpo café claro con puntos oscuros y cola puntiaguda. Vive parcialmente enterrada en fondos arenosos. De comportamiento nocturno y tímido con los buzos.
species.description_en: Serpentine eel with a light brown body dotted with dark spots and a pointed tail. Lives partially buried in sandy bottoms. Nocturnal and shy around divers.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) ulrich zanabria, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by ulrich zanabria

species.description_es: Cangrejo araña de caparazón triangular con bordes marginados y espinas. Color café verdoso. Similar al panchote pero con bordes más marcados. Habita bosques de kelp.
species.description_en: Spider crab with a triangular carapace with ridged margins and spines. Greenish-brown. Similar to the Chilean kelp crab but with more pronounced edges. Inhabits kelp forests.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY), uploaded by Gonzalo Bravo

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Jonatan Ferrada, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Nudibranquio pequeño con cuerpo blanco a amarillento y proyecciones negras en cabeza y cola. Se alimenta de briozoos en rocas y pilotes submarinos. Difícil de ver por su pequeño tamaño.
species.description_en: Small nudibranch with a white to yellowish body and black projections on head and tail. Feeds on bryozoans on rocks and underwater pilings. Hard to spot due to its small size.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Bodo Nuñez Oberg, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Bodo Nuñez Oberg

species.description_es: Cangrejo nadador de color rojo a morado con patas traseras aplanadas como remos. Es activo y agresivo cuando se siente amenazado. Habita fondos arenosos y fangosos subtidales.
species.description_en: Swimming crab, red to purple with flattened paddle-like hind legs. Active and aggressive when threatened. Inhabits sandy and muddy subtidal bottoms.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Lukas Mohr, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC-SA), uploaded by Lukas Mohr

species.description_es: Cangrejo araña aplanado de color café verdoso que se decora con fragmentos de algas para camuflarse. Tiene pinzas con forma de cuchillo. Vive entre algas y kelp del intermareal bajo.
species.description_en: Flat spider crab, greenish-brown, decorating itself with algae fragments for camouflage. Has knife-shaped claws. Lives among algae and kelp in the low intertidal.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Ignacio Contreras, einige Rechte vorbehalten (CC BY-NC), hochgeladen von Ignacio Contreras

species.description_es: Tiburón pequeño de aguas profundas completamente negro con una espina en cada aleta dorsal que puede inyectar veneno. Vive cerca del fondo en la plataforma continental profunda.
species.description_en: Small completely black deep-water shark with a venomous spine on each dorsal fin. Lives near the bottom on the deep continental shelf.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Josefa Araya Campano, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Josefa Araya Campano

species.description_es: Cangrejito diminuto de cuerpo blando que vive dentro de erizos de mar como comensal. La hembra es mucho más grande que el macho. Se alimenta de las partículas que filtra el erizo.
species.description_en: Tiny soft-bodied crab living inside sea urchins as a commensal. Females are much larger than males. Feeds on particles filtered by the urchin.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Diego Almendras G., some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Diego Almendras G.

species.description_es: Cangrejo araña grande de color café rojizo con caparazón triangular cubierto de organismos incrustantes. Patas muy largas y delgadas. Habita fondos profundos de la plataforma continental.
species.description_en: Large spider crab, reddish-brown with a triangular carapace covered in encrusting organisms. Very long, thin legs. Inhabits deep continental shelf bottoms.

primary_photo.attribution:
(c) 
[Photo by L.E.A. Bezerra] Bezerra, L.E.A. & Ribeiro, F.B. (2015). Primitive decapods from the deep sea: first record of blind lobsters (Crustacea: Decapoda: Polychelidae) in northeastern Brazil. Nauplius [online]. v. 23, n. 2 [Accessed 14 November 2021] , some rights reserved (CC BY)

species.description_es: Langosta ciega de aguas profundas con cuerpo aplanado y pinzas largas. Color blanco rosado. Vive enterrada en el fango del fondo abisal. Rara vez capturada y poco conocida.
species.description_en: Blind deep-water lobster with a flattened body and long claws. Pinkish-white. Lives buried in abyssal mud. Rarely captured and little-known.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) MatiasG, some rights reserved (CC BY-ND), uploaded by MatiasG

species.description_es: Cangrejo ermitaño colorido con patas rayadas de rojo y azul que habita conchas grandes. Tiene antenas largas y llamativas. Se encuentra en fondos rocosos sublitorales.
species.description_en: Colorful hermit crab with red and blue striped legs, inhabiting large shells. Has long, eye-catching antennae. Found on subtidal rocky bottoms.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) amutti, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC-ND), uploaded by amutti

species.description_es: Centolla de aguas profundas con caparazón espinoso de color rojo intenso. Tiene patas robustas con crestas. Es pariente de los cangrejos rey y habita fondos profundos del Pacífico sur.
species.description_en: Deep-water king crab relative with a spiny intense red carapace. Has robust legs with ridges. Inhabits deep South Pacific bottoms.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) cstobie, some rights reserved (CC BY), uploaded by cstobie

species.description_es: Quitón grande y oscuro, casi negro, con placas lisas y cinturón ancho con espículas. Se adhiere a rocas intermareales expuestas al oleaje. Uno de los quitones más grandes de Chile.
species.description_en: Large, nearly black chiton with smooth plates and a wide girdle with spicules. Clings to intertidal rocks exposed to wave action. One of Chile's largest chitons.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) jorgelaras, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Alga roja con ramificaciones dicotómicas rígidas de color rojo oscuro. Forma matas compactas en rocas sublitorales. Su textura cartilaginosa la hace resistente al oleaje.
species.description_en: Red alga with rigid dichotomous branching, dark red. Forms compact tufts on subtidal rocks. Its cartilaginous texture makes it resistant to wave action.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Gonzalo Bravo

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Cecilia Paula Alonso, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Alga roja grande con frondas anchas y gruesas de color rojo oscuro. Es la principal fuente de carragenina en Chile. Crece en fondos rocosos sublitorales del sur del país.
species.description_en: Large red alga with broad, thick dark red fronds. Chile's main source of carrageenan. Grows on subtidal rocky bottoms of southern Chile.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) rdelpiano, all rights reserved, uploaded by rdelpiano

species.description_es: Lapa con orificio apical y concha café grisácea con costillas radiales finas. Se encuentra en rocas intermareales expuestas. Raspa algas del sustrato con su rádula.
species.description_en: Limpet with an apical opening and grayish-brown shell with fine radial ribs. Found on exposed intertidal rocks. Scrapes algae from the substrate with its radula.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) maclaudiasegovia, all rights reserved

species.description_es: Caracol pequeño con concha en espiral alargada y escultura espiral. Color café oscuro. Habita fondos fangosos y rocosos en aguas poco profundas de la corriente de Humboldt.
species.description_en: Small snail with an elongated spiral shell and spiral sculpture. Dark brown. Inhabits muddy and rocky bottoms in shallow Humboldt Current waters.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Erasmo Macaya Horta, some rights reserved (CC BY), uploaded by Erasmo Macaya Horta

species.description_es: Alga roja con frondas aplanadas lobuladas de color rojo brillante. Crece en rocas sublitorales en aguas frías del sur de Chile. Tiene una textura suave y membranosa.
species.description_en: Red alga with flat, lobed brilliant red fronds. Grows on subtidal rocks in cold southern Chilean waters. Has a soft, membranous texture.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Diego Almendras G., some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Diego Almendras G.

species.description_es: Cangrejo ermitaño de aguas frías con patas de color naranja rojizo. Habita conchas de caracol en fondos sublitorales del sur de Chile y la Antártica. Poco conocido por buzos.
species.description_en: Cold-water hermit crab with reddish-orange legs. Inhabits snail shells on subtidal bottoms of southern Chile and Antarctica. Little-known to divers.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Marcelo Andrés Rojas González, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Marcelo Andrés Rojas González

species.description_es: Cangrejo ermitaño pequeño con patas de color café rojizo con granulaciones como perlas. Usa conchas vacías como protección. Habita fondos rocosos y arenosos sublitorales.
species.description_en: Small hermit crab with reddish-brown legs with pearl-like granulations. Uses empty shells for protection. Inhabits subtidal rocky and sandy bottoms.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Uriel Sokolowicz Porta - ALEPH, all rights reserved, uploaded by Uriel Sokolowicz Porta - ALEPH

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Gonzalo Roget, some rights reserved (CC BY)
(c) Nicolás Battini, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Cangrejo araña pequeño con caparazón redondeado cubierto de tubérculos. Color café claro. Se camufla con organismos incrustantes y habita fondos de la plataforma continental.
species.description_en: Small spider crab with a rounded carapace covered in tubercles. Light brown. Camouflages with encrusting organisms and inhabits continental shelf bottoms.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Isidora Carvajal, all rights reserved, uploaded by Isidora Carvajal

species.description_es: Camarón pistola pequeño de color verdoso a café que produce un chasquido fuerte con su pinza agrandada. Vive en galerías bajo piedras y entre anémonas. Puede aturdir presas con el sonido.
species.description_en: Small snapping shrimp, greenish to brown, producing a loud snap with its enlarged claw. Lives in burrows under rocks and among anemones. Can stun prey with the sound.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Victor Molina, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Victor Molina

species.description_es: Jaiba de caparazón café rojizo con bordes dentados. Similar a la jaiba reina pero menos común. Se encuentra en fondos rocosos y arenosos de la zona sublitoral.
species.description_en: Crab with a reddish-brown carapace and toothed edges. Similar to the Chilean crab but less common. Found on rocky and sandy sublitoral bottoms.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Gonzalo Bravo

species.description_es: Cangrejo araña con patas extremadamente largas y caparazón triangular pequeño. Color café amarillento. Se mueve lentamente por fondos rocosos profundos donde se camufla entre esponjas.
species.description_en: Spider crab with extremely long legs and a small triangular carapace. Yellowish-brown. Moves slowly across deep rocky bottoms, camouflaging among sponges.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) josefina_paredes, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Cangrejito porcelana con caparazón cubierto de tubérculos redondeados. Color café grisáceo. Se encuentra bajo piedras en el intermareal filtrando plancton del agua.
species.description_en: Porcelain crab with a carapace covered in rounded tubercles. Grayish-brown. Found under rocks in the intertidal zone filtering plankton from the water.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Marcelo Andrés Rojas González, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Marcelo Andrés Rojas González

species.description_es: Pez diminuto y alargado de color café con tres aletas dorsales. Se posa sobre rocas con sus aletas pectorales. Muy territorial a pesar de su pequeño tamaño.
species.description_en: Tiny, elongated brown fish with three dorsal fins. Perches on rocks with its pectoral fins. Very territorial despite its small size.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Leslie Harris, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Leslie Harris

species.description_es: Cangrejito porcelana de color café oscuro con pinzas lisas y aplanadas. Común bajo piedras intermareales donde filtra alimento. Convive con otros cangrejos porcelana.
species.description_en: Dark brown porcelain crab with smooth, flattened claws. Common under intertidal rocks where it filter-feeds. Coexists with other porcelain crabs.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) jorgelaras, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Pez serpentiforme de color negro a café oscuro con textura lisa. Vive en cuevas y grietas de arrecifes profundos. Es el congrio negro, muy apreciado en la gastronomía chilena.
species.description_en: Serpentine fish, black to dark brown with smooth texture. Lives in caves and crevices of deep reefs. The black cusk-eel, highly prized in Chilean gastronomy.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Halvard Aas Midtun, all rights reserved, uploaded by Halvard Aas Midtun

species.description_es: Ofiuro con brazos largos y flexibles que se enrollan alrededor de corales y gorgonias. Color café claro. Habita fondos profundos donde se alimenta de partículas suspendidas.
species.description_en: Brittlestar with long, flexible arms that coil around corals and gorgonians. Light brown. Inhabits deep bottoms where it feeds on suspended particles.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) pabloreyesl, all rights reserved

species.description_es: Pez plateado pequeño similar a la sardina que forma cardúmenes enormes en aguas costeras. Es un recurso pesquero importante en la zona centro-sur de Chile. Base de la cadena alimentaria.
species.description_en: Small silvery sardine-like fish forming enormous schools in coastal waters. An important fishery resource in central-southern Chile. Foundation of the food chain.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) pcaiger, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Pez alargado con hocico en forma de pico y cuerpo plateado. Nada en la superficie y puede saltar fuera del agua. Se encuentra en aguas oceánicas templadas del Pacífico sur.
species.description_en: Elongated fish with a beak-like snout and silvery body. Swims at the surface and can leap out of the water. Found in temperate oceanic waters of the South Pacific.

primary_photo.attribution: (с) pablo_zamora, некоторые права защищены (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Lenguado de ojos grandes con cuerpo aplanado de color café con manchas oscuras. Se mimetiza perfectamente con el fondo arenoso. Depredador de emboscada que espera enterrado en la arena.
species.description_en: Large-eyed flounder with a flattened brown body and dark spots. Camouflages perfectly with the sandy bottom. Ambush predator that waits buried in sand.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) dr. Jackson W.F. Chu, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC-SA), uploaded by dr. Jackson W.F. Chu

species.description_es: Ofiuro con brazos ramificados que se curvan y enrollan. Color blanco a crema. Se alimenta atrapando partículas con sus brazos extendidos. Habita fondos profundos fríos.
species.description_en: Brittlestar with branching arms that curl and coil. White to cream. Feeds by trapping particles with its extended arms. Inhabits cold deep bottoms.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Franco Pérez Valeria, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Franco Pérez Valeria

species.description_es: Pez aplanado lateralmente de color plateado con manchas estrelladas oscuras. No tiene aletas pélvicas. Se alimenta de medusas y ctenóforos en aguas costeras.
species.description_en: Laterally flattened silvery fish with dark star-shaped spots. Lacks pelvic fins. Feeds on jellyfish and ctenophores in coastal waters.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Francisco Riquelme Tapia, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Francisco Riquelme Tapia

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Cecilia Paula Alonso, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Cecilia Paula Alonso, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Cecilia Paula Alonso, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Nudibranquio eólido grande con cuerpo cubierto de ceratas aplanados de color gris a marrón. Se alimenta de anémonas y almacena sus nematocistos. Puede alcanzar un tamaño considerable.
species.description_en: Large aeolid nudibranch with a body covered in flattened gray to brown cerata. Feeds on anemones and stores their nematocysts. Can reach a considerable size.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Gonzalo Bravo

species.description_es: Mantis marina grande con garras raptoras poderosas de color verde a café. Puede golpear con una fuerza increíble para su tamaño. Vive en madrigueras en fondos arenosos y fangosos.
species.description_en: Large mantis shrimp with powerful raptorial claws, green to brown. Can strike with incredible force for its size. Lives in burrows in sandy and muddy bottoms.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) MatiasG, some rights reserved (CC BY-ND), uploaded by MatiasG

species.description_es: Gobio diminuto y transparente que vive asociado a erizos de mar y estrellas. Uno de los peces más pequeños de Chile. Difícil de ver por su tamaño y transparencia.
species.description_en: Tiny, transparent goby that lives associated with sea urchins and starfish. One of Chile's smallest fish. Hard to spot due to its size and transparency.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Erasmo Macaya Horta, some rights reserved (CC BY), uploaded by Erasmo Macaya Horta

species.description_es: Blenio verdoso con manchas irregulares claro-oscuras. Endémico de las islas Juan Fernández y la costa central de Chile. Herbívoro que pasta algas en el intermareal rocoso.
species.description_en: Greenish blenny with irregular light-dark spots. Endemic to the Juan Fernández Islands and central Chilean coast. Herbivore that grazes algae on rocky intertidal.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Erika Mutschke, all rights reserved, uploaded by Erika Mutschke

species.description_es: Estrella de mar pequeña y delgada de color rojo anaranjado con brazos largos y finos. Textura áspera por pequeñas espinas. Habita fondos rocosos profundos donde se alimenta de esponjas.
species.description_en: Small, slender orange-red sea star with long, thin arms. Rough texture from small spines. Inhabits deep rocky bottoms where it feeds on sponges.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) César Pedrini, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by César Pedrini

species.description_es: Pez primitivo sin mandíbulas con cuerpo largo y gelatinoso de color gris rosado. Tiene 14 pares de branquias y produce grandes cantidades de baba como defensa. Un verdadero fósil viviente.
species.description_en: Primitive jawless fish with a long, gelatinous pinkish-gray body. Has 14 pairs of gill openings and produces large amounts of slime for defense. A true living fossil.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Diego Almendras G., some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Diego Almendras G.

species.description_es: Crustáceo excavador similar al cangrejo topo pero más plano y con antenas largas con forma de remo. Se entierra rápidamente en la arena de playas. Sale con las olas para alimentarse.
species.description_en: Burrowing crustacean similar to sand crabs but flatter with long paddle-shaped antennae. Quickly buries in beach sand. Emerges with waves to feed.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) yeungs, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by yeungs

species.description_es: Cangrejo ermitaño con una pinza mucho más grande que la otra, de color café anaranjado. Habita fondos arenosos y fangosos sublitorales donde usa conchas vacías como refugio.
species.description_en: Hermit crab with one claw much larger than the other, orange-brown. Inhabits sandy and muddy subtidal bottoms using empty shells as shelter.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Mariano Rodriguez, all rights reserved, uploaded by Mariano Rodriguez

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Cangrejito diminuto y aplanado que vive dentro de tubos de gusanos poliquetos como comensal. Es casi translúcido y muy difícil de encontrar. Una de las relaciones simbióticas más curiosas.
species.description_en: Tiny, flat crab living inside polychaete worm tubes as a commensal. Almost translucent and very hard to find. One of the most curious symbiotic relationships.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Daniel Stange, einige Rechte vorbehalten (CC BY-NC), hochgeladen von Daniel Stange

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Pintarroja del sur de Chile con cuerpo alargado y manchas marrones oscuras sobre fondo café claro. Similar a S. chilensis pero habita aguas más frías y profundas del sur.
species.description_en: Southern Chilean catshark with an elongated body and dark brown spots on a light brown background. Similar to S. chilensis but inhabits colder, deeper southern waters.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Ignacio Contreras, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Ignacio Contreras

species.description_es: Tiburón de aguas profundas con hocico largo y aplanado. Color café oscuro uniforme. Habita el talud continental y es rara vez visto. Completamente inofensivo y de movimientos lentos.
species.description_en: Deep-water shark with a long, flattened snout. Uniform dark brown. Inhabits the continental slope and is rarely seen. Completely harmless and slow-moving.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Diego Almendras G., some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Diego Almendras G.

species.description_es: Caracol marino con concha robusta ornamentada con nódulos y color café amarillento. Depredador de bivalvos y cirripedios. Se encuentra en fondos rocosos sublitorales.
species.description_en: Marine snail with a robust shell ornamented with nodules, yellowish-brown. Predator of bivalves and barnacles. Found on subtidal rocky bottoms.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Heather Fulton-Bennett, all rights reserved, uploaded by Heather Fulton-Bennett

species.description_es: Alga roja laminar con frondas anchas y textura membranosa. Color rojo oscuro a púrpura. Crece en rocas sublitorales con poca luz. Típica de ambientes sombreados y profundos.
species.description_en: Laminar red alga with broad fronds and membranous texture. Dark red to purple. Grows on subtidal rocks with low light. Typical of shaded and deep environments.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Diego Almendras G., some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Diego Almendras G.

species.description_es: Caracol marino con concha gruesa y esculturada con nódulos y cordones espirales. Color café a gris. Se alimenta de equinodermos en fondos rocosos y arenosos sublitorales.
species.description_en: Marine snail with a thick, sculptured shell with nodules and spiral cords. Brown to gray. Feeds on echinoderms on subtidal rocky and sandy bottoms.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Erasmo Macaya Horta, some rights reserved (CC BY), uploaded by Erasmo Macaya Horta

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Eloísa GI, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC-SA)
(c) Pablo, some rights reserved (CC BY)
(c) Pablo, some rights reserved (CC BY)

species.description_es: Alga roja con ramificaciones compactas y forma de arbusto pequeño. Color rojo oscuro a negro. Crece en el intermareal alto y medio formando tapices sobre las rocas.
species.description_en: Red alga with compact branching forming a small bush shape. Dark red to black. Grows in the upper and mid-intertidal forming mats on rocks.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) nweinzweig, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by nweinzweig

species.description_es: Alga verde que forma cojines gruesos y esponjosos sobre las rocas, de color verde oscuro. Tiene textura aterciopelada al tacto. Muy común en el intermareal medio de Chile.
species.description_en: Green alga forming thick, spongy cushions on rocks, dark green. Has a velvety texture to the touch. Very common in the mid-intertidal of Chile.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Gonzalo Bravo

additional_photos.attribution: (c) Jacobo Martín, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)
(c) Gonzalo Bravo, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Lapa con concha alargada similar a un mejillón, de color café oscuro. Habita costas rocosas del extremo sur de Chile y el archipiélago fueguino. Especie de aguas muy frías.
species.description_en: Limpet with an elongated mussel-like shell, dark brown. Inhabits rocky coasts of extreme southern Chile and the Fuegian archipelago. A cold-water species.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Erasmo Macaya Horta, some rights reserved (CC BY), uploaded by Erasmo Macaya Horta

species.description_es: Alga roja con frondas aplanadas y lobuladas de textura cartilaginosa. Color rojo oscuro. Crece adherida a rocas sublitorales y en pozas intermareales profundas.
species.description_en: Red alga with flat, lobed fronds of cartilaginous texture. Dark red. Grows attached to subtidal rocks and in deep tide pools.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) FELIPE ARIEL FESTER DELGADO, all rights reserved, uploaded by FELIPE ARIEL FESTER DELGADO

species.description_es: Fisurela de concha café con costillas radiales y orificio apical ovalado. Tamaño mediano entre las fisurelas chilenas. Habita rocas intermareales y sublitorales someras.
species.description_en: Keyhole limpet with a brown shell, radial ribs, and oval apical opening. Medium-sized among Chilean keyhole limpets. Inhabits intertidal and shallow subtidal rocks.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Rob Westerduijn, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Rob Westerduijn

species.description_es: Fisurela de concha colorida con tonos rosados y verdes y costillas radiales finas. Como su nombre indica, es una de las más bonitas. Habita el intermareal bajo de Chile central.
species.description_en: Keyhole limpet with a colorful shell in pink and green tones with fine radial ribs. As its name suggests, one of the prettiest. Inhabits the low intertidal of central Chile.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Marcelo matamala, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Marcelo matamala

species.description_es: Pez pequeño y rechoncho con cabeza grande y cuerpo comprimido. Color café moteado. Es la única especie de su familia (Normanichthyidae), endémica del Pacífico sudamericano.
species.description_en: Small, chunky fish with a large head and compressed body. Mottled brown. The sole species in its family (Normanichthyidae), endemic to the South American Pacific.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) dafny, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Alga verde filamentosa con filamentos gruesos y rígidos como cables. Color verde brillante. Crece en pozas intermareales y sobre rocas con buena iluminación.
species.description_en: Filamentous green alga with thick, rigid cable-like filaments. Bright green. Grows in tide pools and on well-lit rocks.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) MatiasG, some rights reserved (CC BY-ND), uploaded by MatiasG

species.description_es: Cangrejo ermitaño peludo con patas cubiertas de vellosidades densas. Color café rojizo. Habita fondos rocosos y arenosos sublitorales usando conchas de varios tamaños.
species.description_en: Hairy hermit crab with legs covered in dense hair. Reddish-brown. Inhabits subtidal rocky and sandy bottoms using shells of various sizes.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Fernando Gracia, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Fernando Gracia

species.description_es: Cangrejito porcelana que vive como comensal sobre erizos de mar, alimentándose de partículas del agua. Es diminuto y se esconde entre las espinas del erizo que lo protegen.
species.description_en: Porcelain crab living as a commensal on sea urchins, feeding on water particles. Tiny and hides among the urchin's protective spines.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Marcelo Andrés Rojas González, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Marcelo Andrés Rojas González

species.description_es: Anémona alargada con columna cubierta de verrugas adhesivas con arena y fragmentos. Tentáculos cortos y numerosos. Se entierra en fondos blandos dejando solo la corona de tentáculos expuesta.
species.description_en: Elongated anemone with column covered in adhesive warts with sand and fragments. Short, numerous tentacles. Buries in soft bottoms leaving only the tentacle crown exposed.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Kalektor, all rights reserved, uploaded by Kalektor

species.description_es: Camarón rosado de aguas profundas con rostro largo y aserrado. Se pesca comercialmente con trampas en la plataforma continental. Conocido como camarón nailón por su textura.
species.description_en: Deep-water pink shrimp with a long, serrated rostrum. Commercially fished with traps on the continental shelf. Known as 'nylon shrimp' for its texture.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) pabloreyesl, all rights reserved

species.description_es: Pez alargado de color café oscuro con manchas claras en el cuerpo. Habita grietas y bajo piedras en arrecifes rocosos poco profundos. Es carnívoro y se alimenta de pequeños invertebrados.
species.description_en: Elongated dark brown fish with light spots on the body. Lives in crevices and under rocks on shallow rocky reefs. Carnivorous, feeding on small invertebrates.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) desireeh, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC)

species.description_es: Alga verde filamentosa que forma matas enmarañadas de filamentos finos y retorcidos. Color verde oscuro. Crece en pozas intermareales y sobre otras algas.
species.description_en: Filamentous green alga forming tangled mats of thin, twisted filaments. Dark green. Grows in tide pools and on other algae.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Dalila Parraguez, some rights reserved (CC BY-NC), uploaded by Dalila Parraguez

species.description_es: Cangrejito porcelana con pinzas muy grandes en relación a su cuerpo. Color café grisáceo. Se encuentra bajo piedras en el intermareal donde filtra partículas del agua.
species.description_en: Porcelain crab with very large claws relative to its body. Grayish-brown. Found under rocks in the intertidal zone, filtering particles from the water.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Maria Angela del Carmen Chiesa Valencia, all rights reserved, uploaded by Maria Angela del Carmen Chiesa Valencia

species.description_es: Cangrejito porcelana con caparazón cubierto de pequeños tubérculos. Color café verdoso. Habita bajo piedras intermareales junto a otras especies de Petrolisthes.
species.description_en: Porcelain crab with a carapace covered in small tubercles. Greenish-brown. Lives under intertidal rocks alongside other Petrolisthes species.

primary_photo.attribution: (c) Uriel Torres Zevallos, all rights reserved, uploaded by Uriel Torres Zevallos
